w:rsidR="00000000" w:rsidDel="00000000" w:rsidP="00000000" w:rsidRDefault="00000000" w:rsidRPr="00000000" w14:paraId="00000002"><w:pPr><w:jc w:val="both"/><w:rPr><w:rFonts w:ascii="EB Garamond" w:cs="EB Garamond" w:eastAsia="EB Garamond" w:hAnsi="EB Garamond"/><w:sz w:val="24"/><w:szCs w:val="24"/></w:rPr></w:pPr><w:r w:rsidDel="00000000" w:rsidR="00000000" w:rsidRPr="00000000"><w:rPr><w:rtl w:val="0"/></w:rPr></w:r></w:p><w:p w:rsidR="00000000" w:rsidDel="00000000" w:rsidP="00000000" w:rsidRDefault="00000000" w:rsidRPr="00000000" w14:paraId="00000003"><w:pPr><w:jc w:val="both"/><w:rPr><w:rFonts w:ascii="EB Garamond" w:cs="EB Garamond" w:eastAsia="EB Garamond" w:hAnsi="EB Garamond"/><w:sz w:val="24"/><w:szCs w:val="24"/></w:rPr></w:pPr><w:r w:rsidDel="00000000" w:rsidR="00000000" w:rsidRPr="00000000"><w:rPr><w:rtl w:val="0"/></w:rPr></w:r></w:p><w:p w:rsidR="00000000" w:rsidDel="00000000" w:rsidP="00000000" w:rsidRDefault="00000000" w:rsidRPr="00000000" w14:paraId="00000004"><w:pPr><w:jc w:val="both"/><w:rPr><w:rFonts w:ascii="EB Garamond" w:cs="EB Garamond" w:eastAsia="EB Garamond" w:hAnsi="EB Garamond"/><w:sz w:val="24"/><w:szCs w:val="24"/></w:rPr></w:pPr><w:r w:rsidDel="00000000" w:rsidR="00000000" w:rsidRPr="00000000"><w:rPr><w:rFonts w:ascii="EB Garamond" w:cs="EB Garamond" w:eastAsia="EB Garamond" w:hAnsi="EB Garamond"/><w:sz w:val="24"/><w:szCs w:val="24"/><w:rtl w:val="0"/></w:rPr><w:t xml:space="preserve">The dark, it has a way of seeping in. It is not merely the absence of light, but the negation of all discernible clarity, a quiet dread that settles, a cold viscosity, in the corners of the consciousness, its metaphysical certainty stretching exactly 1.7 meters behind him, always pausing a breath after he did, a shadow born not of mere obstruction but of pure psychic fact. It waits, patient as a spider in its web, for the moment one allows the primal psychological gravitation to assert its claim: the fear of pure verticality, the terror of standing up without a terrestrial tether, the unbearable loneliness of a soul untethered from the earth. And for young dan, the night had been a long, whispering journey indeed; though light danced in his vision from distant stars, they were the cold a-tonal frequencies of the unmanifest, promising no solace, only the frigid fire of separation and the immutable truth of his solitude.</w:t></w:r></w:p><w:p w:rsidR="00000000" w:rsidDel="00000000" w:rsidP="00000000" w:rsidRDefault="00000000" w:rsidRPr="00000000" w14:paraId="00000005"><w:pPr><w:jc w:val="both"/><w:rPr><w:rFonts w:ascii="EB Garamond" w:cs="EB Garamond" w:eastAsia="EB Garamond" w:hAnsi="EB Garamond"/><w:sz w:val="24"/><w:szCs w:val="24"/></w:rPr></w:pPr><w:r w:rsidDel="00000000" w:rsidR="00000000" w:rsidRPr="00000000"><w:rPr><w:rtl w:val="0"/></w:rPr></w:r></w:p><w:p w:rsidR="00000000" w:rsidDel="00000000" w:rsidP="00000000" w:rsidRDefault="00000000" w:rsidRPr="00000000" w14:paraId="00000006"><w:pPr><w:jc w:val="both"/><w:rPr><w:rFonts w:ascii="EB Garamond" w:cs="EB Garamond" w:eastAsia="EB Garamond" w:hAnsi="EB Garamond"/><w:sz w:val="24"/><w:szCs w:val="24"/></w:rPr></w:pPr><w:r w:rsidDel="00000000" w:rsidR="00000000" w:rsidRPr="00000000"><w:rPr><w:rFonts w:ascii="EB Garamond" w:cs="EB Garamond" w:eastAsia="EB Garamond" w:hAnsi="EB Garamond"/><w:sz w:val="24"/><w:szCs w:val="24"/><w:rtl w:val="0"/></w:rPr><w:t xml:space="preserve">He stirred slowly beneath the thin, scratchy blanket, a vast weariness clutching at him more stubbornly than the receding tendrils of his unseen ascent. The straw beneath his blanket pressed its coarse resistance against his tired bones; a constant, nagging reminder that sleep had been a wrestling match with adversaries that lacked physical form, a brutal contest for the very right to rest. The exhaustion was not the mundane weight of muscle strain—the simple consequence of earth-bound labor, like fetching water or turning the soil with the mechanical expectation of a harvest; this was the deep, marrow-aching sort, a weariness born of a strange, arduous, psychic journey that had demanded a tribute of pure spirit, a depletion that ran deeper than his physical reserves could ever account for. It felt like a sheer mass pressing inward on his bones, a deep density clinging to his marrow, its force lacking the crucial vertical vector of his chosen trajectory, its claim resting entirely on the irresistible lure of the horizontal. This was the cost of choosing the invisible over the visible, a price paid in psychic currency, an immediate and total expenditure of potential energy for a gain that could not be mapped. The entire night felt like a period of system 2 processing, the slow, painful work of calculating a trajectory that defied all observable physics, leaving his body depleted by the spirit's relentless, uphill labor against the fundamental, ancient law that says all things must lay down and be still. For in his dream, his consciousness, nimble as a fox through thorny undergrowth, had achieved psychic decoupling, leaving his earthly vessel far below, straining toward heights that defied the conventional, earth-bound concept of 'up'—a desperate verticality against the universal, horizontal claim. That invisible ascent, stretching his inner vision toward the glinting pinpricks of distant stars, had rendered his spirit exsanguinated by the perpetual friction—the sheer cost of choosing up—and his body, consequently, mirrored that overwhelming fatigue. His spirit felt depleted, like a threadbare tunic worn thin by constant rubbing, and his body, consequently, mirrored that fatigue with a heavy, protesting sigh. He blinked against the shy, dim light that crept, cautious as a thief, through the small window, squinting as if to scrub away the lingering haze still clinging stubbornly to his thoughts. The room smelled faintly of dust and ancient, cool masonry, a pervasive scent of stagnation, of ages refusing to move on, an air that seemed to press down on him with the cumulative weight of all forgotten history. He had not truly rested. He thought of his father, aviel, still in hebron, and knew this weariness was a taste of what aviel had endured for years; an overwhelming specific gravity of aviel's rot, the physical refusal to exchange potential energy for upward velocity—a drain of pure volition far more debilitating than any physical exertion, a sinking into the grave while the heart still beat, a choice that traded all future momentum for present certainty.</w:t></w:r></w:p><w:p w:rsidR="00000000" w:rsidDel="00000000" w:rsidP="00000000" w:rsidRDefault="00000000" w:rsidRPr="00000000" w14:paraId="00000007"><w:pPr><w:jc w:val="both"/><w:rPr><w:rFonts w:ascii="EB Garamond" w:cs="EB Garamond" w:eastAsia="EB Garamond" w:hAnsi="EB Garamond"/><w:sz w:val="24"/><w:szCs w:val="24"/></w:rPr></w:pPr><w:r w:rsidDel="00000000" w:rsidR="00000000" w:rsidRPr="00000000"><w:rPr><w:rtl w:val="0"/></w:rPr></w:r></w:p><w:p w:rsidR="00000000" w:rsidDel="00000000" w:rsidP="00000000" w:rsidRDefault="00000000" w:rsidRPr="00000000" w14:paraId="00000008"><w:pPr><w:jc w:val="both"/><w:rPr><w:rFonts w:ascii="EB Garamond" w:cs="EB Garamond" w:eastAsia="EB Garamond" w:hAnsi="EB Garamond"/><w:sz w:val="24"/><w:szCs w:val="24"/></w:rPr></w:pPr><w:r w:rsidDel="00000000" w:rsidR="00000000" w:rsidRPr="00000000"><w:rPr><w:rFonts w:ascii="EB Garamond" w:cs="EB Garamond" w:eastAsia="EB Garamond" w:hAnsi="EB Garamond"/><w:sz w:val="24"/><w:szCs w:val="24"/><w:rtl w:val="0"/></w:rPr><w:t xml:space="preserve">Dan threw his legs off the edge of the cot, a low groan escaping his lips, his muscles protesting the loss of vertical momentum from the night’s unrest. He dressed quickly, feeling the persistent chill of the masonry room seep into his skin like a silent complaint. He wrapped his rough wool cloak tighter as he moved towards the doorway, understanding that the chill was not just air, but the essential condition of the world he had chosen to enter, a temperature that measured the proximity of the unmanifested truth. Outside, the first blush of dawn began to settle softly over the rooftops of bethlehem, its color severe and unsentimental, spreading like frigid honey across the distant hills with a fleeting, golden promise that he immediately distrusted, for all promises of comfort were snares designed by the low gods. The sun, a shy, tentative golden orb, was still hugging the eastern horizon, its light spreading like spilled honey that refused to dry, a vision of false abundance that only intensified his spiritual hunger.</w:t></w:r></w:p><w:p w:rsidR="00000000" w:rsidDel="00000000" w:rsidP="00000000" w:rsidRDefault="00000000" w:rsidRPr="00000000" w14:paraId="00000009"><w:pPr><w:jc w:val="both"/><w:rPr><w:rFonts w:ascii="EB Garamond" w:cs="EB Garamond" w:eastAsia="EB Garamond" w:hAnsi="EB Garamond"/><w:sz w:val="24"/><w:szCs w:val="24"/></w:rPr></w:pPr><w:r w:rsidDel="00000000" w:rsidR="00000000" w:rsidRPr="00000000"><w:rPr><w:rtl w:val="0"/></w:rPr></w:r></w:p><w:p w:rsidR="00000000" w:rsidDel="00000000" w:rsidP="00000000" w:rsidRDefault="00000000" w:rsidRPr="00000000" w14:paraId="0000000A"><w:pPr><w:jc w:val="both"/><w:rPr><w:rFonts w:ascii="EB Garamond" w:cs="EB Garamond" w:eastAsia="EB Garamond" w:hAnsi="EB Garamond"/><w:sz w:val="24"/><w:szCs w:val="24"/></w:rPr></w:pPr><w:r w:rsidDel="00000000" w:rsidR="00000000" w:rsidRPr="00000000"><w:rPr><w:rFonts w:ascii="EB Garamond" w:cs="EB Garamond" w:eastAsia="EB Garamond" w:hAnsi="EB Garamond"/><w:sz w:val="24"/><w:szCs w:val="24"/><w:rtl w:val="0"/></w:rPr><w:t xml:space="preserve">He held a direction in his consciousness, clear and unwavering as the sky overhead. His journey led him north, toward the boundary complexity of the jebusite lands—a place he had only glimpsed in the strange, shimmering tapestry of dreams, a territory defined by a chaos that was more honest than the false order of his old life. The road ahead would be long; its hours stretching thin and quiet, measured only by the steady rhythm of his determined ambulation; a low, constant murmuring in his spirit, the noise of resistance against the silent, magnetic pull of the earth, an invisible tether constantly pulling at his ankles.</w:t></w:r></w:p><w:p w:rsidR="00000000" w:rsidDel="00000000" w:rsidP="00000000" w:rsidRDefault="00000000" w:rsidRPr="00000000" w14:paraId="0000000B"><w:pPr><w:jc w:val="both"/><w:rPr><w:rFonts w:ascii="EB Garamond" w:cs="EB Garamond" w:eastAsia="EB Garamond" w:hAnsi="EB Garamond"/><w:sz w:val="24"/><w:szCs w:val="24"/></w:rPr></w:pPr><w:r w:rsidDel="00000000" w:rsidR="00000000" w:rsidRPr="00000000"><w:rPr><w:rtl w:val="0"/></w:rPr></w:r></w:p><w:p w:rsidR="00000000" w:rsidDel="00000000" w:rsidP="00000000" w:rsidRDefault="00000000" w:rsidRPr="00000000" w14:paraId="0000000C"><w:pPr><w:jc w:val="both"/><w:rPr><w:rFonts w:ascii="EB Garamond" w:cs="EB Garamond" w:eastAsia="EB Garamond" w:hAnsi="EB Garamond"/><w:sz w:val="24"/><w:szCs w:val="24"/></w:rPr></w:pPr><w:r w:rsidDel="00000000" w:rsidR="00000000" w:rsidRPr="00000000"><w:rPr><w:rFonts w:ascii="EB Garamond" w:cs="EB Garamond" w:eastAsia="EB Garamond" w:hAnsi="EB Garamond"/><w:sz w:val="24"/><w:szCs w:val="24"/><w:rtl w:val="0"/></w:rPr><w:t xml:space="preserve">The way led him first through the highlands of gilo, a place where the air grew thin and crisp, sharp enough to make his lungs ache with exhilaration, a sensation he welcomed as a confirmation of his velocity. He passed ancient watchtowers, like silent sentinels carved from the very rock, structures that had witnessed the failure of countless generations to ascend. Below, a vast valley stretched, a place whispered about in hushed tones, where the very soil seemed to hum with old, forgotten stories—a gravity well for the settling of horizontal grief, a natural space for the accumulation of collective human sorrow and the debris of failed ambitions.</w:t></w:r></w:p><w:p w:rsidR="00000000" w:rsidDel="00000000" w:rsidP="00000000" w:rsidRDefault="00000000" w:rsidRPr="00000000" w14:paraId="0000000D"><w:pPr><w:jc w:val="both"/><w:rPr><w:rFonts w:ascii="EB Garamond" w:cs="EB Garamond" w:eastAsia="EB Garamond" w:hAnsi="EB Garamond"/><w:sz w:val="24"/><w:szCs w:val="24"/></w:rPr></w:pPr><w:r w:rsidDel="00000000" w:rsidR="00000000" w:rsidRPr="00000000"><w:rPr><w:rtl w:val="0"/></w:rPr></w:r></w:p><w:p w:rsidR="00000000" w:rsidDel="00000000" w:rsidP="00000000" w:rsidRDefault="00000000" w:rsidRPr="00000000" w14:paraId="0000000E"><w:pPr><w:jc w:val="both"/><w:rPr><w:rFonts w:ascii="EB Garamond" w:cs="EB Garamond" w:eastAsia="EB Garamond" w:hAnsi="EB Garamond"/><w:sz w:val="24"/><w:szCs w:val="24"/></w:rPr></w:pPr><w:r w:rsidDel="00000000" w:rsidR="00000000" w:rsidRPr="00000000"><w:rPr><w:rFonts w:ascii="EB Garamond" w:cs="EB Garamond" w:eastAsia="EB Garamond" w:hAnsi="EB Garamond"/><w:sz w:val="24"/><w:szCs w:val="24"/><w:rtl w:val="0"/></w:rPr><w:t xml:space="preserve">He moved steadily onward, hour after hour, his physical volition becoming a constant, painful expenditure of energy. The familiar, soft earth of bethlehem yielded to more masonry, rougher and more defiant. The scent of dry earth, warm and comforting, now mingled with the sharp, wild tang of unknown rituals carried on the breeze; the odor of sacred viscosity that tasted like iron dust on the back of his throat, a warning that the low gods were defined by their material consumption and the blood payment they demanded for the lie of rest. He pushed past the metaphorical karman line—the threshold where the atmospheric pull of his old world yielded to the vacuum of his ascent, a point of no psychological return where the commitment became absolute.</w:t></w:r></w:p><w:p w:rsidR="00000000" w:rsidDel="00000000" w:rsidP="00000000" w:rsidRDefault="00000000" w:rsidRPr="00000000" w14:paraId="0000000F"><w:pPr><w:jc w:val="both"/><w:rPr><w:rFonts w:ascii="EB Garamond" w:cs="EB Garamond" w:eastAsia="EB Garamond" w:hAnsi="EB Garamond"/><w:sz w:val="24"/><w:szCs w:val="24"/></w:rPr></w:pPr><w:r w:rsidDel="00000000" w:rsidR="00000000" w:rsidRPr="00000000"><w:rPr><w:rtl w:val="0"/></w:rPr></w:r></w:p><w:p w:rsidR="00000000" w:rsidDel="00000000" w:rsidP="00000000" w:rsidRDefault="00000000" w:rsidRPr="00000000" w14:paraId="00000010"><w:pPr><w:jc w:val="both"/><w:rPr><w:rFonts w:ascii="EB Garamond" w:cs="EB Garamond" w:eastAsia="EB Garamond" w:hAnsi="EB Garamond"/><w:sz w:val="24"/><w:szCs w:val="24"/></w:rPr></w:pPr><w:r w:rsidDel="00000000" w:rsidR="00000000" w:rsidRPr="00000000"><w:rPr><w:rFonts w:ascii="EB Garamond" w:cs="EB Garamond" w:eastAsia="EB Garamond" w:hAnsi="EB Garamond"/><w:sz w:val="24"/><w:szCs w:val="24"/><w:rtl w:val="0"/></w:rPr><w:t xml:space="preserve">And then, there it was: jebus, a formidable silhouette emerging from the morning haze, growing more distinct and imposing with every stride. The settlement, perched atop its towering cliffs and jagged rock faces like a predator on its lofty nest, was not only a place, but a statement—a colossal monument built on a wilful refusal of grace, a self-made tower of babel where human will was spent horizontally in an effort to conquer the sky, conquering the height not for the spirit, but for the flesh. He had refused astarte’s sensual temptation, whose beauty was meant to lure him into a lesser contentment. Now he saw an entire metropolis that had built itself upon such a temptation—a sprawling, rock-carved snare, its walls unnervingly a geometry of sharp angles that mimicked, but failed to achieve, the symmetry of flesh, a design that elicited a primal, defensive tension in the gut because it was too close to the divine, yet fundamentally wrong. The structures were built for stability, demanding a constant tribute of human focus and energy to maintain their high position; they were a collective, physical effort to prove that the earth itself could be a heaven, provided enough volition was spent horizontally, a profound, tragic miscalculation of moral geometry. The air felt heavy with the scent of unacknowledged competition, the zero-sum game of their belief system where every blessing received meant a curse cast elsewhere, a theological argument inscribed into the rock itself: the low gods are the only gods, for they are the only ones whose weight you can truly feel and whose works you can measure with a surveyor’s chain.</w:t></w:r></w:p><w:p w:rsidR="00000000" w:rsidDel="00000000" w:rsidP="00000000" w:rsidRDefault="00000000" w:rsidRPr="00000000" w14:paraId="00000011"><w:pPr><w:jc w:val="both"/><w:rPr><w:rFonts w:ascii="EB Garamond" w:cs="EB Garamond" w:eastAsia="EB Garamond" w:hAnsi="EB Garamond"/><w:sz w:val="24"/><w:szCs w:val="24"/></w:rPr></w:pPr><w:r w:rsidDel="00000000" w:rsidR="00000000" w:rsidRPr="00000000"><w:rPr><w:rtl w:val="0"/></w:rPr></w:r></w:p><w:p w:rsidR="00000000" w:rsidDel="00000000" w:rsidP="00000000" w:rsidRDefault="00000000" w:rsidRPr="00000000" w14:paraId="00000012"><w:pPr><w:jc w:val="both"/><w:rPr><w:rFonts w:ascii="EB Garamond" w:cs="EB Garamond" w:eastAsia="EB Garamond" w:hAnsi="EB Garamond"/><w:sz w:val="24"/><w:szCs w:val="24"/></w:rPr></w:pPr><w:r w:rsidDel="00000000" w:rsidR="00000000" w:rsidRPr="00000000"><w:rPr><w:rFonts w:ascii="EB Garamond" w:cs="EB Garamond" w:eastAsia="EB Garamond" w:hAnsi="EB Garamond"/><w:sz w:val="24"/><w:szCs w:val="24"/><w:rtl w:val="0"/></w:rPr><w:t xml:space="preserve">He entered the settlement proper through a gate, a dark, gaping maw carved directly into the rock face itself, swallowing the morning light as a beast swallows its prey. The streets were a dizzying ascent, a spiraling trajectory carved directly into the very hillside, forcing the supplicant to climb simply to stand still. The air inside was dense, heavy, a shroud woven from the cloying, sweet scent of incense, the sharp tang of human sweat, and the acrid, earthy odor of animal waste, a sensory manifestation of spiritual viscosity. The entire place felt dense, layered, built not only on the hill but of it, a living, breathing monument to its low gods. It reminded him of dagon, who was not a god of the sky or the open sea, but whose power lay entirely in the compressed, suffocating volume of the lowlands.</w:t></w:r></w:p><w:p w:rsidR="00000000" w:rsidDel="00000000" w:rsidP="00000000" w:rsidRDefault="00000000" w:rsidRPr="00000000" w14:paraId="00000013"><w:pPr><w:jc w:val="both"/><w:rPr><w:rFonts w:ascii="EB Garamond" w:cs="EB Garamond" w:eastAsia="EB Garamond" w:hAnsi="EB Garamond"/><w:sz w:val="24"/><w:szCs w:val="24"/></w:rPr></w:pPr><w:r w:rsidDel="00000000" w:rsidR="00000000" w:rsidRPr="00000000"><w:rPr><w:rtl w:val="0"/></w:rPr></w:r></w:p><w:p w:rsidR="00000000" w:rsidDel="00000000" w:rsidP="00000000" w:rsidRDefault="00000000" w:rsidRPr="00000000" w14:paraId="00000014"><w:pPr><w:jc w:val="both"/><w:rPr><w:rFonts w:ascii="EB Garamond" w:cs="EB Garamond" w:eastAsia="EB Garamond" w:hAnsi="EB Garamond"/><w:sz w:val="24"/><w:szCs w:val="24"/></w:rPr></w:pPr><w:r w:rsidDel="00000000" w:rsidR="00000000" w:rsidRPr="00000000"><w:rPr><w:rFonts w:ascii="EB Garamond" w:cs="EB Garamond" w:eastAsia="EB Garamond" w:hAnsi="EB Garamond"/><w:sz w:val="24"/><w:szCs w:val="24"/><w:rtl w:val="0"/></w:rPr><w:t xml:space="preserve">He needed to find the way towards the highest ground. He asked a passing townsman for directions. “just keep north,” the townsman said with a tired smile, “and the heights will guide you. they say the powers live up there still, watching. always watching.” (The townsman offered a direction, but not the truth, for no one in this urban core dared to be truly curious about the internal war raging between flesh and spirit, preferring the solid chains of the low gods to the terrifying freedom of the high ones, trading a terrifying potential for a manageable certainty.)</w:t></w:r></w:p><w:p w:rsidR="00000000" w:rsidDel="00000000" w:rsidP="00000000" w:rsidRDefault="00000000" w:rsidRPr="00000000" w14:paraId="00000015"><w:pPr><w:jc w:val="both"/><w:rPr><w:rFonts w:ascii="EB Garamond" w:cs="EB Garamond" w:eastAsia="EB Garamond" w:hAnsi="EB Garamond"/><w:sz w:val="24"/><w:szCs w:val="24"/></w:rPr></w:pPr><w:r w:rsidDel="00000000" w:rsidR="00000000" w:rsidRPr="00000000"><w:rPr><w:rtl w:val="0"/></w:rPr></w:r></w:p><w:p w:rsidR="00000000" w:rsidDel="00000000" w:rsidP="00000000" w:rsidRDefault="00000000" w:rsidRPr="00000000" w14:paraId="00000016"><w:pPr><w:jc w:val="both"/><w:rPr><w:rFonts w:ascii="EB Garamond" w:cs="EB Garamond" w:eastAsia="EB Garamond" w:hAnsi="EB Garamond"/><w:sz w:val="24"/><w:szCs w:val="24"/></w:rPr></w:pPr><w:r w:rsidDel="00000000" w:rsidR="00000000" w:rsidRPr="00000000"><w:rPr><w:rFonts w:ascii="EB Garamond" w:cs="EB Garamond" w:eastAsia="EB Garamond" w:hAnsi="EB Garamond"/><w:sz w:val="24"/><w:szCs w:val="24"/><w:rtl w:val="0"/></w:rPr><w:t xml:space="preserve">As he climbed higher, the air, now cleaner, filled his lungs with a sharp, invigorating clarity that sharpened his thoughts. The view opened up behind him—the urban core he had left shrinking into a cluster of tiny rooftops, a vast, complex geometry reduced to a simple symbol of material accumulation. A deep emptiness settled inside him; not of loss, which would imply that something of value had been left behind, but of clarified separation, a space emptied to receive a new, frigid kind of pressure. It was a hunger only the northern road could feed, a psychological demand that began not with his feet but with his very soul, a need for an unmovable constant of the highest law to anchor his reality, a law that transcended the fickle rules of the low gods.</w:t></w:r></w:p><w:p w:rsidR="00000000" w:rsidDel="00000000" w:rsidP="00000000" w:rsidRDefault="00000000" w:rsidRPr="00000000" w14:paraId="00000017"><w:pPr><w:jc w:val="both"/><w:rPr><w:rFonts w:ascii="EB Garamond" w:cs="EB Garamond" w:eastAsia="EB Garamond" w:hAnsi="EB Garamond"/><w:sz w:val="24"/><w:szCs w:val="24"/></w:rPr></w:pPr><w:r w:rsidDel="00000000" w:rsidR="00000000" w:rsidRPr="00000000"><w:rPr><w:rtl w:val="0"/></w:rPr></w:r></w:p><w:p w:rsidR="00000000" w:rsidDel="00000000" w:rsidP="00000000" w:rsidRDefault="00000000" w:rsidRPr="00000000" w14:paraId="00000018"><w:pPr><w:jc w:val="both"/><w:rPr><w:rFonts w:ascii="EB Garamond" w:cs="EB Garamond" w:eastAsia="EB Garamond" w:hAnsi="EB Garamond"/><w:sz w:val="24"/><w:szCs w:val="24"/></w:rPr></w:pPr><w:r w:rsidDel="00000000" w:rsidR="00000000" w:rsidRPr="00000000"><w:rPr><w:rFonts w:ascii="EB Garamond" w:cs="EB Garamond" w:eastAsia="EB Garamond" w:hAnsi="EB Garamond"/><w:sz w:val="24"/><w:szCs w:val="24"/><w:rtl w:val="0"/></w:rPr><w:t xml:space="preserve">He passed through the northern gate, jebus simply a necessary stop, a stepping stone on a much longer journey, a point of density to be traversed, not inhabited. The mountain’s rugged embrace finally loosened its grip as he left the dense urban core behind. The way led him across a winding ridge and into rolling hills and small, gentle valleys. Shepherds’ calls echoed across the quiet, sun-drenched landscape, a timeless sound that grounded him with the simple, brutal reality of pastoral life—the constant, repetitive horizontal effort of managing flocks. He skirted small, silent outposts manned by a few soldiers whose faces were etched with the same weary cynicism. The hours flowed, slow and golden, until the imposing silhouette of gibeah finally came into view. As he entered the town, he was met with a scene of jarring chaos. The streets were filled with distraught people, their faces twisted with grief and raw, guttural anger, their frantic movements a dance of mourning. The lamentation was thick, a suffocating shroud, a dreadful symphony of wails and cries directed at the indifferent sky. The raw emotion was a kind of viscous drag on his own sense of momentum, a psychological barrier that took a measurable expenditure of effort to move through.</w:t></w:r></w:p><w:p w:rsidR="00000000" w:rsidDel="00000000" w:rsidP="00000000" w:rsidRDefault="00000000" w:rsidRPr="00000000" w14:paraId="00000019"><w:pPr><w:jc w:val="both"/><w:rPr><w:rFonts w:ascii="EB Garamond" w:cs="EB Garamond" w:eastAsia="EB Garamond" w:hAnsi="EB Garamond"/><w:sz w:val="24"/><w:szCs w:val="24"/></w:rPr></w:pPr><w:r w:rsidDel="00000000" w:rsidR="00000000" w:rsidRPr="00000000"><w:rPr><w:rtl w:val="0"/></w:rPr></w:r></w:p><w:p w:rsidR="00000000" w:rsidDel="00000000" w:rsidP="00000000" w:rsidRDefault="00000000" w:rsidRPr="00000000" w14:paraId="0000001A"><w:pPr><w:jc w:val="both"/><w:rPr><w:rFonts w:ascii="EB Garamond" w:cs="EB Garamond" w:eastAsia="EB Garamond" w:hAnsi="EB Garamond"/><w:sz w:val="24"/><w:szCs w:val="24"/></w:rPr></w:pPr><w:r w:rsidDel="00000000" w:rsidR="00000000" w:rsidRPr="00000000"><w:rPr><w:rFonts w:ascii="EB Garamond" w:cs="EB Garamond" w:eastAsia="EB Garamond" w:hAnsi="EB Garamond"/><w:sz w:val="24"/><w:szCs w:val="24"/><w:rtl w:val="0"/></w:rPr><w:t xml:space="preserve">A beggar, his clothes in rags, his face a roadmap of hunger and despair, reached out a skeletal hand. &quot;alms, traveler? For the love of the absolute good, a tribute to fill this hollow space?&quot; The man's desperation was a mirror of aviel's own gnawing, unfillable void, merely expressed through poverty rather than hoarded wealth.</w:t></w:r></w:p><w:p w:rsidR="00000000" w:rsidDel="00000000" w:rsidP="00000000" w:rsidRDefault="00000000" w:rsidRPr="00000000" w14:paraId="0000001B"><w:pPr><w:jc w:val="both"/><w:rPr><w:rFonts w:ascii="EB Garamond" w:cs="EB Garamond" w:eastAsia="EB Garamond" w:hAnsi="EB Garamond"/><w:sz w:val="24"/><w:szCs w:val="24"/></w:rPr></w:pPr><w:r w:rsidDel="00000000" w:rsidR="00000000" w:rsidRPr="00000000"><w:rPr><w:rtl w:val="0"/></w:rPr></w:r></w:p><w:p w:rsidR="00000000" w:rsidDel="00000000" w:rsidP="00000000" w:rsidRDefault="00000000" w:rsidRPr="00000000" w14:paraId="0000001C"><w:pPr><w:jc w:val="both"/><w:rPr><w:rFonts w:ascii="EB Garamond" w:cs="EB Garamond" w:eastAsia="EB Garamond" w:hAnsi="EB Garamond"/><w:sz w:val="24"/><w:szCs w:val="24"/></w:rPr></w:pPr><w:r w:rsidDel="00000000" w:rsidR="00000000" w:rsidRPr="00000000"><w:rPr><w:rFonts w:ascii="EB Garamond" w:cs="EB Garamond" w:eastAsia="EB Garamond" w:hAnsi="EB Garamond"/><w:sz w:val="24"/><w:szCs w:val="24"/><w:rtl w:val="0"/></w:rPr><w:t xml:space="preserve">Dan's heart felt a frigid, physical contraction in his chest as he gave the man a coin. &quot;blessed be,&quot; he croaked, then added, almost to himself, &quot;i pray our next true leader is as gentle as you. oh, how we yearn for a king! a true king!&quot; dan understood. The yearning for a king was the same hunger as his father's; a desperate reaching for a horizontal solution, a political snare—the total and immediate refusal to fight the internal war of friction and ascent. It was the tragic loss aversion of a populace, choosing the pain of a known master over the terror of freedom, a theological failure that mirrored the ancient demand for a mortal ruler, a willful rejection of the unseen sovereign in favor of a tangible, accountable mass of flesh and crown. The chaos and sorrow of the town, reeling from the loss of its temporal leader, was a testament to the void created by this choice.</w:t></w:r></w:p><w:p w:rsidR="00000000" w:rsidDel="00000000" w:rsidP="00000000" w:rsidRDefault="00000000" w:rsidRPr="00000000" w14:paraId="0000001D"><w:pPr><w:jc w:val="both"/><w:rPr><w:rFonts w:ascii="EB Garamond" w:cs="EB Garamond" w:eastAsia="EB Garamond" w:hAnsi="EB Garamond"/><w:sz w:val="24"/><w:szCs w:val="24"/></w:rPr></w:pPr><w:r w:rsidDel="00000000" w:rsidR="00000000" w:rsidRPr="00000000"><w:rPr><w:rtl w:val="0"/></w:rPr></w:r></w:p><w:p w:rsidR="00000000" w:rsidDel="00000000" w:rsidP="00000000" w:rsidRDefault="00000000" w:rsidRPr="00000000" w14:paraId="0000001E"><w:pPr><w:jc w:val="both"/><w:rPr><w:rFonts w:ascii="EB Garamond" w:cs="EB Garamond" w:eastAsia="EB Garamond" w:hAnsi="EB Garamond"/><w:sz w:val="24"/><w:szCs w:val="24"/></w:rPr></w:pPr><w:r w:rsidDel="00000000" w:rsidR="00000000" w:rsidRPr="00000000"><w:rPr><w:rFonts w:ascii="EB Garamond" w:cs="EB Garamond" w:eastAsia="EB Garamond" w:hAnsi="EB Garamond"/><w:sz w:val="24"/><w:szCs w:val="24"/><w:rtl w:val="0"/></w:rPr><w:t xml:space="preserve">He reached bethel as the last of the sun's light faded, a town that, unlike only jebus or gibeah, did not feel like a place he was passing through, but one he might be absorbed by. Its air was lighter, a cool, invisible current of purified separation that rippled through him, a deceptive melody of peace. The town's aura was not peaceful; it felt hushed, a silence that seemed to hold its breath. The very masonry seemed to glow with an inner light, a soft, inviting promise of earthen heaven, a momentary, localized break in the universal law of friction that felt both perfect and perfectly dangerous.</w:t></w:r></w:p><w:p w:rsidR="00000000" w:rsidDel="00000000" w:rsidP="00000000" w:rsidRDefault="00000000" w:rsidRPr="00000000" w14:paraId="0000001F"><w:pPr><w:jc w:val="both"/><w:rPr><w:rFonts w:ascii="EB Garamond" w:cs="EB Garamond" w:eastAsia="EB Garamond" w:hAnsi="EB Garamond"/><w:sz w:val="24"/><w:szCs w:val="24"/></w:rPr></w:pPr><w:r w:rsidDel="00000000" w:rsidR="00000000" w:rsidRPr="00000000"><w:rPr><w:rtl w:val="0"/></w:rPr></w:r></w:p><w:p w:rsidR="00000000" w:rsidDel="00000000" w:rsidP="00000000" w:rsidRDefault="00000000" w:rsidRPr="00000000" w14:paraId="00000020"><w:pPr><w:jc w:val="both"/><w:rPr><w:rFonts w:ascii="EB Garamond" w:cs="EB Garamond" w:eastAsia="EB Garamond" w:hAnsi="EB Garamond"/><w:sz w:val="24"/><w:szCs w:val="24"/></w:rPr></w:pPr><w:r w:rsidDel="00000000" w:rsidR="00000000" w:rsidRPr="00000000"><w:rPr><w:rFonts w:ascii="EB Garamond" w:cs="EB Garamond" w:eastAsia="EB Garamond" w:hAnsi="EB Garamond"/><w:sz w:val="24"/><w:szCs w:val="24"/><w:rtl w:val="0"/></w:rPr><w:t xml:space="preserve">He walked into the heart of the town. His body was a symphony of aches, his throat parched, but he was more aware of his exhaustion now, as a separate thing from his volition, a physical body that demanded tribute after its spirit had flown too high. He found a well and drank deeply, the coolness a shock to his system. He sat down and watched the people. They seemed to be always looking up; their gazes aimed at the gathering stars, their movements marked by a quiet, purposeful calm, a counterpoint to the chaotic wails of gibeah.</w:t></w:r></w:p><w:p w:rsidR="00000000" w:rsidDel="00000000" w:rsidP="00000000" w:rsidRDefault="00000000" w:rsidRPr="00000000" w14:paraId="00000021"><w:pPr><w:jc w:val="both"/><w:rPr><w:rFonts w:ascii="EB Garamond" w:cs="EB Garamond" w:eastAsia="EB Garamond" w:hAnsi="EB Garamond"/><w:sz w:val="24"/><w:szCs w:val="24"/></w:rPr></w:pPr><w:r w:rsidDel="00000000" w:rsidR="00000000" w:rsidRPr="00000000"><w:rPr><w:rtl w:val="0"/></w:rPr></w:r></w:p><w:p w:rsidR="00000000" w:rsidDel="00000000" w:rsidP="00000000" w:rsidRDefault="00000000" w:rsidRPr="00000000" w14:paraId="00000022"><w:pPr><w:jc w:val="both"/><w:rPr><w:rFonts w:ascii="EB Garamond" w:cs="EB Garamond" w:eastAsia="EB Garamond" w:hAnsi="EB Garamond"/><w:sz w:val="24"/><w:szCs w:val="24"/></w:rPr></w:pPr><w:r w:rsidDel="00000000" w:rsidR="00000000" w:rsidRPr="00000000"><w:rPr><w:rFonts w:ascii="EB Garamond" w:cs="EB Garamond" w:eastAsia="EB Garamond" w:hAnsi="EB Garamond"/><w:sz w:val="24"/><w:szCs w:val="24"/><w:rtl w:val="0"/></w:rPr><w:t xml:space="preserve">It was then he noticed her. An old woman, with a face like a dried riverbed, etched with countless stories. She offered him food. As he ate, she spoke, her gaze returning to the sky, layering her wisdom directly onto the shared act of survival. &quot;the wind blows through an empty house, my son,&quot; she mused, her voice soft, &quot;but if the house is cluttered with things, the wind finds only obstacles. the light has to be given a space to shine.&quot; He thought of his father’s house, filled with hoarded refuse, and the empty, vast sky he had been traveling toward, understanding that emptiness was not a lack, but a necessary precondition for ascent.</w:t></w:r></w:p><w:p w:rsidR="00000000" w:rsidDel="00000000" w:rsidP="00000000" w:rsidRDefault="00000000" w:rsidRPr="00000000" w14:paraId="00000023"><w:pPr><w:jc w:val="both"/><w:rPr><w:rFonts w:ascii="EB Garamond" w:cs="EB Garamond" w:eastAsia="EB Garamond" w:hAnsi="EB Garamond"/><w:sz w:val="24"/><w:szCs w:val="24"/></w:rPr></w:pPr><w:r w:rsidDel="00000000" w:rsidR="00000000" w:rsidRPr="00000000"><w:rPr><w:rtl w:val="0"/></w:rPr></w:r></w:p><w:p w:rsidR="00000000" w:rsidDel="00000000" w:rsidP="00000000" w:rsidRDefault="00000000" w:rsidRPr="00000000" w14:paraId="00000024"><w:pPr><w:jc w:val="both"/><w:rPr><w:rFonts w:ascii="EB Garamond" w:cs="EB Garamond" w:eastAsia="EB Garamond" w:hAnsi="EB Garamond"/><w:sz w:val="24"/><w:szCs w:val="24"/></w:rPr></w:pPr><w:r w:rsidDel="00000000" w:rsidR="00000000" w:rsidRPr="00000000"><w:rPr><w:rFonts w:ascii="EB Garamond" w:cs="EB Garamond" w:eastAsia="EB Garamond" w:hAnsi="EB Garamond"/><w:sz w:val="24"/><w:szCs w:val="24"/><w:rtl w:val="0"/></w:rPr><w:t xml:space="preserve">&quot;you carry a thing of hope,&quot; she said, her eyes meeting his once more. &quot;but hope,&quot; she continued, &quot;is merely a wish, a shallow thing on the surface that things might play out in your favor. it is an expectation of a return, a contract with the future.&quot; She reached into a small satchel at her volition and produced a smaller, smooth rock, a bit of carnelian that glowed with a deep, fiery red. She placed it in his hand, a weight of surprising substance. &quot;here is a thing of faith. for faith is the deep knowing, down to the core of your being, that all things will work out, in everyone's favor, by time’s end, whether you are present to see it or not. it is a promise of no return.&quot; Her words were an echo of the bhagavad gita, a mandate to act without attachment to outcome, to perform the duty of the spirit regardless of the profit to the body.</w:t></w:r></w:p><w:p w:rsidR="00000000" w:rsidDel="00000000" w:rsidP="00000000" w:rsidRDefault="00000000" w:rsidRPr="00000000" w14:paraId="00000025"><w:pPr><w:jc w:val="both"/><w:rPr><w:rFonts w:ascii="EB Garamond" w:cs="EB Garamond" w:eastAsia="EB Garamond" w:hAnsi="EB Garamond"/><w:sz w:val="24"/><w:szCs w:val="24"/></w:rPr></w:pPr><w:r w:rsidDel="00000000" w:rsidR="00000000" w:rsidRPr="00000000"><w:rPr><w:rtl w:val="0"/></w:rPr></w:r></w:p><w:p w:rsidR="00000000" w:rsidDel="00000000" w:rsidP="00000000" w:rsidRDefault="00000000" w:rsidRPr="00000000" w14:paraId="00000026"><w:pPr><w:jc w:val="both"/><w:rPr><w:rFonts w:ascii="EB Garamond" w:cs="EB Garamond" w:eastAsia="EB Garamond" w:hAnsi="EB Garamond"/><w:sz w:val="24"/><w:szCs w:val="24"/></w:rPr></w:pPr><w:r w:rsidDel="00000000" w:rsidR="00000000" w:rsidRPr="00000000"><w:rPr><w:rFonts w:ascii="EB Garamond" w:cs="EB Garamond" w:eastAsia="EB Garamond" w:hAnsi="EB Garamond"/><w:sz w:val="24"/><w:szCs w:val="24"/><w:rtl w:val="0"/></w:rPr><w:t xml:space="preserve">He stayed in bethel, drawn in by her presence. He felt himself settling, the desperate energy of his unseen ascent slowly replaced by a vast peace, a feeling of being home. Yet he felt a subtle dissonance: the townspeople were rooted deeply to the ground; their comfort a chain tethering them to the very thing they sought to transcend. This was a place of deep contentment, but it was a contentment of the body, and the luminous lie, a state of psychological inertia—not the absolute truth of separation. They had solved the problem of human suffering by making a perfect, manageable earth out of their heaven. This was the most perfect creation of a lesser architect, a place that solved the human condition by perfecting the tether, a gentle trap built from good intentions and quiet certainty.</w:t></w:r></w:p><w:p w:rsidR="00000000" w:rsidDel="00000000" w:rsidP="00000000" w:rsidRDefault="00000000" w:rsidRPr="00000000" w14:paraId="00000027"><w:pPr><w:jc w:val="both"/><w:rPr><w:rFonts w:ascii="EB Garamond" w:cs="EB Garamond" w:eastAsia="EB Garamond" w:hAnsi="EB Garamond"/><w:sz w:val="24"/><w:szCs w:val="24"/></w:rPr></w:pPr><w:r w:rsidDel="00000000" w:rsidR="00000000" w:rsidRPr="00000000"><w:rPr><w:rtl w:val="0"/></w:rPr></w:r></w:p><w:p w:rsidR="00000000" w:rsidDel="00000000" w:rsidP="00000000" w:rsidRDefault="00000000" w:rsidRPr="00000000" w14:paraId="00000028"><w:pPr><w:jc w:val="both"/><w:rPr><w:rFonts w:ascii="EB Garamond" w:cs="EB Garamond" w:eastAsia="EB Garamond" w:hAnsi="EB Garamond"/><w:sz w:val="24"/><w:szCs w:val="24"/></w:rPr></w:pPr><w:r w:rsidDel="00000000" w:rsidR="00000000" w:rsidRPr="00000000"><w:rPr><w:rFonts w:ascii="EB Garamond" w:cs="EB Garamond" w:eastAsia="EB Garamond" w:hAnsi="EB Garamond"/><w:sz w:val="24"/><w:szCs w:val="24"/><w:rtl w:val="0"/></w:rPr><w:t xml:space="preserve">When dawn, a soft whisper of pink and gold, returned to the sky for the third time, the realization came to him, as cold and sharp as the north air. The quiet hum of bethel, the vast sense of belonging with the woman, was the softest, deadliest snare of all. He felt a fierce, aching longing to stay, to simply be, to settle, to finally accept the warmth. He saw a fleeting image of his father’s chaotic house, his mother’s death, and aviel’s overwhelming horizontal pull, and understood that the pull of bethel was the same force, only disguised in robes of virtue. The feeling was a vast psychological gravitation toward rest, the ultimate seduction. The choice was not between evil and good, but between the absolute good and the unmanifested truth, and he knew he had to choose the latter, the frigid, less comfortable way, the way that promised only the continuation of friction.</w:t></w:r></w:p><w:p w:rsidR="00000000" w:rsidDel="00000000" w:rsidP="00000000" w:rsidRDefault="00000000" w:rsidRPr="00000000" w14:paraId="00000029"><w:pPr><w:jc w:val="both"/><w:rPr><w:rFonts w:ascii="EB Garamond" w:cs="EB Garamond" w:eastAsia="EB Garamond" w:hAnsi="EB Garamond"/><w:sz w:val="24"/><w:szCs w:val="24"/></w:rPr></w:pPr><w:r w:rsidDel="00000000" w:rsidR="00000000" w:rsidRPr="00000000"><w:rPr><w:rtl w:val="0"/></w:rPr></w:r></w:p><w:p w:rsidR="00000000" w:rsidDel="00000000" w:rsidP="00000000" w:rsidRDefault="00000000" w:rsidRPr="00000000" w14:paraId="0000002A"><w:pPr><w:jc w:val="both"/><w:rPr><w:rFonts w:ascii="EB Garamond" w:cs="EB Garamond" w:eastAsia="EB Garamond" w:hAnsi="EB Garamond"/><w:sz w:val="24"/><w:szCs w:val="24"/></w:rPr></w:pPr><w:r w:rsidDel="00000000" w:rsidR="00000000" w:rsidRPr="00000000"><w:rPr><w:rFonts w:ascii="EB Garamond" w:cs="EB Garamond" w:eastAsia="EB Garamond" w:hAnsi="EB Garamond"/><w:sz w:val="24"/><w:szCs w:val="24"/><w:rtl w:val="0"/></w:rPr><w:t xml:space="preserve">The sharp, physical ache of a soul being rended settled in the marrow of his chest—this cost was the living sacrifice, a demand for the total and immediate tribute of his momentary peace. It was a psychological mass that was exponentially more crushing than the gravitational constant of the old life he had shed, for he was not leaving something bad, but something perfectly, beautifully good. His consciousness experienced a moment of temporal distortion where decades collapsed into the single realization of the counterfeit's perfect, false world, and he understood the finality of his departure.</w:t></w:r></w:p><w:p w:rsidR="00000000" w:rsidDel="00000000" w:rsidP="00000000" w:rsidRDefault="00000000" w:rsidRPr="00000000" w14:paraId="0000002B"><w:pPr><w:jc w:val="both"/><w:rPr><w:rFonts w:ascii="EB Garamond" w:cs="EB Garamond" w:eastAsia="EB Garamond" w:hAnsi="EB Garamond"/><w:sz w:val="24"/><w:szCs w:val="24"/></w:rPr></w:pPr><w:r w:rsidDel="00000000" w:rsidR="00000000" w:rsidRPr="00000000"><w:rPr><w:rtl w:val="0"/></w:rPr></w:r></w:p><w:p w:rsidR="00000000" w:rsidDel="00000000" w:rsidP="00000000" w:rsidRDefault="00000000" w:rsidRPr="00000000" w14:paraId="0000002C"><w:pPr><w:jc w:val="both"/><w:rPr><w:rFonts w:ascii="EB Garamond" w:cs="EB Garamond" w:eastAsia="EB Garamond" w:hAnsi="EB Garamond"/><w:sz w:val="24"/><w:szCs w:val="24"/></w:rPr></w:pPr><w:r w:rsidDel="00000000" w:rsidR="00000000" w:rsidRPr="00000000"><w:rPr><w:rFonts w:ascii="EB Garamond" w:cs="EB Garamond" w:eastAsia="EB Garamond" w:hAnsi="EB Garamond"/><w:sz w:val="24"/><w:szCs w:val="24"/><w:rtl w:val="0"/></w:rPr><w:t xml:space="preserve">His hands instinctively closed around the two smooth rocks. Their presence was not comfort, but a perfect, precarious thermodynamic tension against the raw ache: the mother’s coolness sinking dan’s excess earth-heat, and the woman’s warmth radiating the memory of the life he was refusing to fully relinquish. This was the final price: the failure of language to fully contain a truth so vast—and the phantom ache in his tongue returned, heavy and numb, confirming the final, irreversible nature of his choice, the payment of the ultimate toll for volition.</w:t></w:r></w:p><w:p w:rsidR="00000000" w:rsidDel="00000000" w:rsidP="00000000" w:rsidRDefault="00000000" w:rsidRPr="00000000" w14:paraId="0000002D"><w:pPr><w:jc w:val="both"/><w:rPr><w:rFonts w:ascii="EB Garamond" w:cs="EB Garamond" w:eastAsia="EB Garamond" w:hAnsi="EB Garamond"/><w:sz w:val="24"/><w:szCs w:val="24"/></w:rPr></w:pPr><w:r w:rsidDel="00000000" w:rsidR="00000000" w:rsidRPr="00000000"><w:rPr><w:rtl w:val="0"/></w:rPr></w:r></w:p><w:p w:rsidR="00000000" w:rsidDel="00000000" w:rsidP="00000000" w:rsidRDefault="00000000" w:rsidRPr="00000000" w14:paraId="0000002E"><w:pPr><w:jc w:val="both"/><w:rPr><w:rFonts w:ascii="EB Garamond" w:cs="EB Garamond" w:eastAsia="EB Garamond" w:hAnsi="EB Garamond"/><w:sz w:val="24"/><w:szCs w:val="24"/></w:rPr></w:pPr><w:r w:rsidDel="00000000" w:rsidR="00000000" w:rsidRPr="00000000"><w:rPr><w:rFonts w:ascii="EB Garamond" w:cs="EB Garamond" w:eastAsia="EB Garamond" w:hAnsi="EB Garamond"/><w:sz w:val="24"/><w:szCs w:val="24"/><w:rtl w:val="0"/></w:rPr><w:t xml:space="preserve">He walked out through the gate, the pace of his walk slow, deliberate, each step costing a measurable, physical unit of will; the cost of the living sacrifice immediately visible in his diminished horizontal velocity. He looked up at the sky, at the first streaks of dawn, and his lips formed the words, &quot;but this is not high enough.&quot; It was the ultimate statement of volition, the rejection of all comfort. The vast ache of heartbreak settled within him—the absolute value of the tribute required by the ascent.</w:t></w:r></w:p><w:p w:rsidR="00000000" w:rsidDel="00000000" w:rsidP="00000000" w:rsidRDefault="00000000" w:rsidRPr="00000000" w14:paraId="0000002F"><w:pPr><w:jc w:val="both"/><w:rPr><w:rFonts w:ascii="EB Garamond" w:cs="EB Garamond" w:eastAsia="EB Garamond" w:hAnsi="EB Garamond"/><w:sz w:val="24"/><w:szCs w:val="24"/></w:rPr></w:pPr><w:r w:rsidDel="00000000" w:rsidR="00000000" w:rsidRPr="00000000"><w:rPr><w:rtl w:val="0"/></w:rPr></w:r></w:p><w:p w:rsidR="00000000" w:rsidDel="00000000" w:rsidP="00000000" w:rsidRDefault="00000000" w:rsidRPr="00000000" w14:paraId="00000030"><w:pPr><w:jc w:val="both"/><w:rPr><w:rFonts w:ascii="EB Garamond" w:cs="EB Garamond" w:eastAsia="EB Garamond" w:hAnsi="EB Garamond"/><w:sz w:val="24"/><w:szCs w:val="24"/></w:rPr></w:pPr><w:r w:rsidDel="00000000" w:rsidR="00000000" w:rsidRPr="00000000"><w:rPr><w:rFonts w:ascii="EB Garamond" w:cs="EB Garamond" w:eastAsia="EB Garamond" w:hAnsi="EB Garamond"/><w:sz w:val="24"/><w:szCs w:val="24"/><w:rtl w:val="0"/></w:rPr><w:t xml:space="preserve">The silence that closed around him was not the simple quiet of the dawn, but a deep, resonant hum—the perfect static confirming the vast, frigid distance between the earth and the unmanifest—leaving him to walk on, alone, toward a higher, cooler truth: the only thing more demanding than fighting the low gods is refusing the peace offered by the absolute good.</w:t></w:r></w:p><w:sectPr><w:pgSz w:h="15840" w:w="12240" w:orient="portrait"/><w:pgMar w:bottom="1440" w:top="1440" w:left="1440" w:right="1440" w:header="720" w:footer="720"/><w:pgNumType w:start="1"/></w:sectPr></w:body></w:document>

xmlns:wp="http://schemas.openxmlformats.org/drawingml/2006/wordprocessingDrawing" xmlns:w10="urn:schemas-microsoft-com:office:word" xmlns:w="http://schemas.openxmlformats.org/wordprocessingml/2006/main" xmlns:wne="http://schemas.microsoft.com/office/word/2006/wordml" xmlns:w14="http://schemas.microsoft.com/office/word/2010/wordml" xmlns:w15="http://schemas.microsoft.com/office/word/2012/wordml" xmlns:w16="http://schemas.microsoft.com/office/word/2018/wordml" xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/office/tasks/2019/documenttasks" xmlns:cr="http://schemas.microsoft.com/office/comments/2020/reactions"><w:body><w:p w:rsidR="00000000" w:rsidDel="00000000" w:rsidP="00000000" w:rsidRDefault="00000000" w:rsidRPr="00000000" w14:paraId="00000001"><w:pPr><w:pStyle w:val="Title"/><w:rPr><w:rFonts w:ascii="EB Garamond" w:cs="EB Garamond" w:eastAsia="EB Garamond" w:hAnsi="EB Garamond"/><w:sz w:val="28"/><w:szCs w:val="28"/></w:rPr></w:pPr><w:bookmarkStart w:colFirst="0" w:colLast="0" w:name="_jnpie2ci9u9g" w:id="0"/><w:bookmarkEnd w:id="0"/><w:r w:rsidDel="00000000" w:rsidR="00000000" w:rsidRPr="00000000"><w:rPr><w:rFonts w:ascii="EB Garamond" w:cs="EB Garamond" w:eastAsia="EB Garamond" w:hAnsi="EB Garamond"/><w:sz w:val="28"/><w:szCs w:val="28"/><w:rtl w:val="0"/></w:rPr><w:t xml:space="preserve">CHAPTER 1: STARDUST TO STARDUST</w:t></w:r></w:p><w:p w:rsidR="00000000" w:rsidDel="00000000" w:rsidP="00000000" w:rsidRDefault="00000000" w:rsidRPr="00000000" w14:paraId="00000002"><w:pPr><w:rPr><w:rFonts w:ascii="EB Garamond" w:cs="EB Garamond" w:eastAsia="EB Garamond" w:hAnsi="EB Garamond"/><w:color w:val="0000ff"/><w:sz w:val="24"/><w:szCs w:val="24"/></w:rPr></w:pPr><w:r w:rsidDel="00000000" w:rsidR="00000000" w:rsidRPr="00000000"><w:rPr><w:rtl w:val="0"/></w:rPr></w:r></w:p><w:p w:rsidR="00000000" w:rsidDel="00000000" w:rsidP="00000000" w:rsidRDefault="00000000" w:rsidRPr="00000000" w14:paraId="00000003"><w:pPr><w:rPr><w:rFonts w:ascii="EB Garamond" w:cs="EB Garamond" w:eastAsia="EB Garamond" w:hAnsi="EB Garamond"/><w:color w:val="0000ff"/><w:sz w:val="24"/><w:szCs w:val="24"/></w:rPr></w:pPr><w:r w:rsidDel="00000000" w:rsidR="00000000" w:rsidRPr="00000000"><w:rPr><w:rtl w:val="0"/></w:rPr></w:r></w:p><w:p w:rsidR="00000000" w:rsidDel="00000000" w:rsidP="00000000" w:rsidRDefault="00000000" w:rsidRPr="00000000" w14:paraId="00000004"><w:pPr><w:rPr><w:rFonts w:ascii="EB Garamond" w:cs="EB Garamond" w:eastAsia="EB Garamond" w:hAnsi="EB Garamond"/><w:color w:val="0000ff"/><w:sz w:val="24"/><w:szCs w:val="24"/></w:rPr></w:pPr><w:r w:rsidDel="00000000" w:rsidR="00000000" w:rsidRPr="00000000"><w:rPr><w:rtl w:val="0"/></w:rPr></w:r></w:p><w:p w:rsidR="00000000" w:rsidDel="00000000" w:rsidP="00000000" w:rsidRDefault="00000000" w:rsidRPr="00000000" w14:paraId="00000005"><w:pPr><w:rPr><w:rFonts w:ascii="EB Garamond" w:cs="EB Garamond" w:eastAsia="EB Garamond" w:hAnsi="EB Garamond"/><w:sz w:val="24"/><w:szCs w:val="24"/></w:rPr></w:pPr><w:r w:rsidDel="00000000" w:rsidR="00000000" w:rsidRPr="00000000"><w:rPr><w:rFonts w:ascii="EB Garamond" w:cs="EB Garamond" w:eastAsia="EB Garamond" w:hAnsi="EB Garamond"/><w:sz w:val="24"/><w:szCs w:val="24"/><w:rtl w:val="0"/></w:rPr><w:t xml:space="preserve">A four-room house in Hebron had become the shape of one man's heart, divided against itself — not metaphor but architecture: the western chamber sealed against the eastern, the northern passage choked with seven years of accumulated refusal, each room a record of what remains when grief mistakes itself for patience and patience mistakes itself for virtue. You might ask how a dwelling comes to resemble something as treacherous as a divided heart — such a question, posed while reading this, means you and I will get along. I tell you this as one who was present at that threshold on the night this story begins, and who remained long after the boy inside had gone to places no torch can follow. What was actually happening had been building for seven years, compounding the way a debt compounds: invisibly, then all at once.</w:t></w:r></w:p><w:p w:rsidR="00000000" w:rsidDel="00000000" w:rsidP="00000000" w:rsidRDefault="00000000" w:rsidRPr="00000000" w14:paraId="00000006"><w:pPr><w:rPr><w:rFonts w:ascii="EB Garamond" w:cs="EB Garamond" w:eastAsia="EB Garamond" w:hAnsi="EB Garamond"/><w:sz w:val="24"/><w:szCs w:val="24"/></w:rPr></w:pPr><w:r w:rsidDel="00000000" w:rsidR="00000000" w:rsidRPr="00000000"><w:rPr><w:rtl w:val="0"/></w:rPr></w:r></w:p><w:p w:rsidR="00000000" w:rsidDel="00000000" w:rsidP="00000000" w:rsidRDefault="00000000" w:rsidRPr="00000000" w14:paraId="00000007"><w:pPr><w:rPr><w:rFonts w:ascii="EB Garamond" w:cs="EB Garamond" w:eastAsia="EB Garamond" w:hAnsi="EB Garamond"/><w:sz w:val="24"/><w:szCs w:val="24"/></w:rPr></w:pPr><w:r w:rsidDel="00000000" w:rsidR="00000000" w:rsidRPr="00000000"><w:rPr><w:rFonts w:ascii="EB Garamond" w:cs="EB Garamond" w:eastAsia="EB Garamond" w:hAnsi="EB Garamond"/><w:sz w:val="24"/><w:szCs w:val="24"/><w:rtl w:val="0"/></w:rPr><w:t xml:space="preserve">Father Aviel had been a scholar, sharp as fresh-knapped flint, sought and summoned by those whose questions twisted beyond their own unraveling. When Mother Zuna passed — nameless there forevermore — something stopped within him: not death, which would have been the cleaner mercy, but the stillness of a cart whose axle has been shattered so completely that no carpenter in the province can name the right repair. The gods he had gathered from the western plains — brittle bronze forms of forgotten flame, of fading fortune, of fertility that promised and did not deliver — could not fill what had gone hollow in him. So he swept them away and kept only one: the lord of limitless dust and earthly plenty, who whispered that hoarded objects might hold back time himself, just to spite whatever kept making new things. That one stayed. That one, as you will discover, was paying close attention all along.</w:t></w:r></w:p><w:p w:rsidR="00000000" w:rsidDel="00000000" w:rsidP="00000000" w:rsidRDefault="00000000" w:rsidRPr="00000000" w14:paraId="00000008"><w:pPr><w:rPr><w:rFonts w:ascii="EB Garamond" w:cs="EB Garamond" w:eastAsia="EB Garamond" w:hAnsi="EB Garamond"/><w:sz w:val="24"/><w:szCs w:val="24"/></w:rPr></w:pPr><w:r w:rsidDel="00000000" w:rsidR="00000000" w:rsidRPr="00000000"><w:rPr><w:rtl w:val="0"/></w:rPr></w:r></w:p><w:p w:rsidR="00000000" w:rsidDel="00000000" w:rsidP="00000000" w:rsidRDefault="00000000" w:rsidRPr="00000000" w14:paraId="00000009"><w:pPr><w:rPr><w:rFonts w:ascii="EB Garamond" w:cs="EB Garamond" w:eastAsia="EB Garamond" w:hAnsi="EB Garamond"/><w:sz w:val="24"/><w:szCs w:val="24"/></w:rPr></w:pPr><w:r w:rsidDel="00000000" w:rsidR="00000000" w:rsidRPr="00000000"><w:rPr><w:rFonts w:ascii="EB Garamond" w:cs="EB Garamond" w:eastAsia="EB Garamond" w:hAnsi="EB Garamond"/><w:sz w:val="24"/><w:szCs w:val="24"/><w:rtl w:val="0"/></w:rPr><w:t xml:space="preserve">On a night when the moon rode full on its third day, the son moved through the monument his father's madness had made. They had called him Dan once, when names still mattered in this dwelling that hardly stood. He was sixteen, with kind eyes that held more sorrow than a boy should carry and unruly black hair that wrestled with his simple commandments. He had started at the hearth: crouching low, striking flint against pyrite twice before the sparks scattered in a brief gold cascade and the pitch-soaked rag caught, guttering once in protest before it committed. Now the frail flame trembled not from wind but from the staccato percussion against his ribs, casting suspicious shadows across the narrow corridor. Each barefoot step through the refuse was a wager against sound: one shard of pottery, one brittle bone breaking beneath his weight, and the house would know. He was moving toward his father's room, and tonight he would enter the dreamscape to accomplish what no daylight conversation had been able to.</w:t></w:r></w:p><w:p w:rsidR="00000000" w:rsidDel="00000000" w:rsidP="00000000" w:rsidRDefault="00000000" w:rsidRPr="00000000" w14:paraId="0000000A"><w:pPr><w:rPr><w:rFonts w:ascii="EB Garamond" w:cs="EB Garamond" w:eastAsia="EB Garamond" w:hAnsi="EB Garamond"/><w:sz w:val="24"/><w:szCs w:val="24"/></w:rPr></w:pPr><w:r w:rsidDel="00000000" w:rsidR="00000000" w:rsidRPr="00000000"><w:rPr><w:rtl w:val="0"/></w:rPr></w:r></w:p><w:p w:rsidR="00000000" w:rsidDel="00000000" w:rsidP="00000000" w:rsidRDefault="00000000" w:rsidRPr="00000000" w14:paraId="0000000B"><w:pPr><w:rPr><w:rFonts w:ascii="EB Garamond" w:cs="EB Garamond" w:eastAsia="EB Garamond" w:hAnsi="EB Garamond"/><w:sz w:val="24"/><w:szCs w:val="24"/></w:rPr></w:pPr><w:r w:rsidDel="00000000" w:rsidR="00000000" w:rsidRPr="00000000"><w:rPr><w:rFonts w:ascii="EB Garamond" w:cs="EB Garamond" w:eastAsia="EB Garamond" w:hAnsi="EB Garamond"/><w:sz w:val="24"/><w:szCs w:val="24"/><w:rtl w:val="0"/></w:rPr><w:t xml:space="preserve">The corridor choked with debris that had long since stopped being merely debris. Broken pottery lay scattered like the shards of failed offerings — Dan had counted eighteen visible pieces one morning before stopping, unable to push past that number. He would not have been able to say why eighteen stopped him. He simply stopped. Fragments of parchment and brittle wax tablets, once bearing records of lineage and covenant, lay stripped clean by mold that had developed what could only be called ambition — spreading in patterns too deliberate for accident, following lines that suggested a map of something no one had commissioned and no one sane would consult. Ruined brass astrolabes gathered in the shadowed corners, their rings seized into arrangements no craftsman had designed, pointing with insistent consistency toward a direction that corresponded to no coordinate in heaven or on earth. Desiccated fruits clung to their seeds in the manner of things that insist they are still alive while offering nothing by way of evidence. From the darkest pocket of the corridor — the corner the torch's light did not quite reach — something made no sound, but in a manner that was different from ordinary silence. The distinction was slight. Dan noticed it the way you notice that a room has someone in it before you find the someone.</w:t></w:r></w:p><w:p w:rsidR="00000000" w:rsidDel="00000000" w:rsidP="00000000" w:rsidRDefault="00000000" w:rsidRPr="00000000" w14:paraId="0000000C"><w:pPr><w:rPr><w:rFonts w:ascii="EB Garamond" w:cs="EB Garamond" w:eastAsia="EB Garamond" w:hAnsi="EB Garamond"/><w:sz w:val="24"/><w:szCs w:val="24"/></w:rPr></w:pPr><w:r w:rsidDel="00000000" w:rsidR="00000000" w:rsidRPr="00000000"><w:rPr><w:rtl w:val="0"/></w:rPr></w:r></w:p><w:p w:rsidR="00000000" w:rsidDel="00000000" w:rsidP="00000000" w:rsidRDefault="00000000" w:rsidRPr="00000000" w14:paraId="0000000D"><w:pPr><w:rPr><w:rFonts w:ascii="EB Garamond" w:cs="EB Garamond" w:eastAsia="EB Garamond" w:hAnsi="EB Garamond"/><w:sz w:val="24"/><w:szCs w:val="24"/></w:rPr></w:pPr><w:r w:rsidDel="00000000" w:rsidR="00000000" w:rsidRPr="00000000"><w:rPr><w:rFonts w:ascii="EB Garamond" w:cs="EB Garamond" w:eastAsia="EB Garamond" w:hAnsi="EB Garamond"/><w:sz w:val="24"/><w:szCs w:val="24"/><w:rtl w:val="0"/></w:rPr><w:t xml:space="preserve">The smell came in layers. Ammonia sharp enough to sear the passages and raise involuntary water to the eyes. Decay: sweet and oppressive as overripe fruit three days past its reckoning. Dusty herbs that had surrendered their virtue and become merely powder with the memory of virtue. Maggots worked the rotted cloth in the near dark — pale as mushroom flesh, fat as thumbs — the only living things in this corridor that had found their purpose and intended to keep it, as purposeful as priests at a rite no one had thought to cancel. Flies rose and fell in low, patient clouds; their droning was the liturgy of the house, as established and particular as any psalm. Beneath everything, the mineral reek of disturbed earth, of things buried being pulled back into light where they had no business being. Dan had stopped noticing any of this six years ago. The fact that he noticed it again tonight told him something about the weight of what he was about to attempt.</w:t></w:r></w:p><w:p w:rsidR="00000000" w:rsidDel="00000000" w:rsidP="00000000" w:rsidRDefault="00000000" w:rsidRPr="00000000" w14:paraId="0000000E"><w:pPr><w:rPr><w:rFonts w:ascii="EB Garamond" w:cs="EB Garamond" w:eastAsia="EB Garamond" w:hAnsi="EB Garamond"/><w:sz w:val="24"/><w:szCs w:val="24"/></w:rPr></w:pPr><w:r w:rsidDel="00000000" w:rsidR="00000000" w:rsidRPr="00000000"><w:rPr><w:rtl w:val="0"/></w:rPr></w:r></w:p><w:p w:rsidR="00000000" w:rsidDel="00000000" w:rsidP="00000000" w:rsidRDefault="00000000" w:rsidRPr="00000000" w14:paraId="0000000F"><w:pPr><w:rPr><w:rFonts w:ascii="EB Garamond" w:cs="EB Garamond" w:eastAsia="EB Garamond" w:hAnsi="EB Garamond"/><w:sz w:val="24"/><w:szCs w:val="24"/></w:rPr></w:pPr><w:r w:rsidDel="00000000" w:rsidR="00000000" w:rsidRPr="00000000"><w:rPr><w:rFonts w:ascii="EB Garamond" w:cs="EB Garamond" w:eastAsia="EB Garamond" w:hAnsi="EB Garamond"/><w:sz w:val="24"/><w:szCs w:val="24"/><w:rtl w:val="0"/></w:rPr><w:t xml:space="preserve">Here is what the record seldom clarifies, and what you deserve to understand before we go further: some few children come into the world able to remain awake inside the country where all dreamers travel. Not awake the way the body is awake — no muscle moves, no eyelid shifts — but present with the full attention of a watcher who cannot be deceived about what is actually happening. Dan's mother had been such a traveler, and she had begun teaching him when he was still young enough to learn without fully understanding what was being handed to him. To enter another dreamer's country required proximity — the closer the watcher, the cleaner the passage, and this was why Dan moved toward his father's door rather than attempting the work from his own bed. The method was simple enough to describe and requires more discipline than most adults possess: release yesterday, letting it dissolve the way salt dissolves in deep water; release tomorrow, unhooking from its obligations; then release now — this is the difficult one — until the body achieves the stillness of deep water and what is true may surface in it. He had practiced this for five years in secret, in this house. Tonight the practice had a destination.</w:t></w:r></w:p><w:p w:rsidR="00000000" w:rsidDel="00000000" w:rsidP="00000000" w:rsidRDefault="00000000" w:rsidRPr="00000000" w14:paraId="00000010"><w:pPr><w:rPr><w:rFonts w:ascii="EB Garamond" w:cs="EB Garamond" w:eastAsia="EB Garamond" w:hAnsi="EB Garamond"/><w:sz w:val="24"/><w:szCs w:val="24"/></w:rPr></w:pPr><w:r w:rsidDel="00000000" w:rsidR="00000000" w:rsidRPr="00000000"><w:rPr><w:rtl w:val="0"/></w:rPr></w:r></w:p><w:p w:rsidR="00000000" w:rsidDel="00000000" w:rsidP="00000000" w:rsidRDefault="00000000" w:rsidRPr="00000000" w14:paraId="00000011"><w:pPr><w:rPr><w:rFonts w:ascii="EB Garamond" w:cs="EB Garamond" w:eastAsia="EB Garamond" w:hAnsi="EB Garamond"/><w:sz w:val="24"/><w:szCs w:val="24"/></w:rPr></w:pPr><w:r w:rsidDel="00000000" w:rsidR="00000000" w:rsidRPr="00000000"><w:rPr><w:rFonts w:ascii="EB Garamond" w:cs="EB Garamond" w:eastAsia="EB Garamond" w:hAnsi="EB Garamond"/><w:sz w:val="24"/><w:szCs w:val="24"/><w:rtl w:val="0"/></w:rPr><w:t xml:space="preserve">Dan arrived at his father's door and stopped. He pressed his back to the wall beside it and went still — not the still of patience of serpents in a garden, but the particular stillness of someone listening through stone for a sound they need to hear. From the other side came the sound of a man trying to outrun his own breath: that ragged, straining inhalation belonging to the body running drills for a crisis that has not yet arrived or has long since passed. Dan waited through one long stretch of this, then another. The breath gradually deepened, steadied, lost its urgency. Then, finally — the slow and heavy pull of a man gone fully under. His father was down. He was not running anymore.</w:t></w:r></w:p><w:p w:rsidR="00000000" w:rsidDel="00000000" w:rsidP="00000000" w:rsidRDefault="00000000" w:rsidRPr="00000000" w14:paraId="00000012"><w:pPr><w:rPr><w:rFonts w:ascii="EB Garamond" w:cs="EB Garamond" w:eastAsia="EB Garamond" w:hAnsi="EB Garamond"/><w:sz w:val="24"/><w:szCs w:val="24"/></w:rPr></w:pPr><w:r w:rsidDel="00000000" w:rsidR="00000000" w:rsidRPr="00000000"><w:rPr><w:rtl w:val="0"/></w:rPr></w:r></w:p><w:p w:rsidR="00000000" w:rsidDel="00000000" w:rsidP="00000000" w:rsidRDefault="00000000" w:rsidRPr="00000000" w14:paraId="00000013"><w:pPr><w:rPr><w:rFonts w:ascii="EB Garamond" w:cs="EB Garamond" w:eastAsia="EB Garamond" w:hAnsi="EB Garamond"/><w:sz w:val="24"/><w:szCs w:val="24"/></w:rPr></w:pPr><w:r w:rsidDel="00000000" w:rsidR="00000000" w:rsidRPr="00000000"><w:rPr><w:rFonts w:ascii="EB Garamond" w:cs="EB Garamond" w:eastAsia="EB Garamond" w:hAnsi="EB Garamond"/><w:sz w:val="24"/><w:szCs w:val="24"/><w:rtl w:val="0"/></w:rPr><w:t xml:space="preserve">He extinguished the torch against the wall.</w:t></w:r></w:p><w:p w:rsidR="00000000" w:rsidDel="00000000" w:rsidP="00000000" w:rsidRDefault="00000000" w:rsidRPr="00000000" w14:paraId="00000014"><w:pPr><w:rPr><w:rFonts w:ascii="EB Garamond" w:cs="EB Garamond" w:eastAsia="EB Garamond" Until the body became still water. Garamond"/><w:sz w:val="24"/><w:szCs w:val="24"/></w:rPr></w:pPr><w:r w:rsidDel="00000000" w:rsidR="00000000" w:rsidRPr="00000000"><w:rPr><w:rFonts w:ascii="EB Garamond" w:cs="EB Garamond" country that has been waiting longer than waiting has a name. The formless dark churned below and the void was total above, as things must have been before the first word of the first morning was spoken into the dark that had not yet known it was waiting. Dan oriented quickly — he knew this country the way a man knows the house he grew up in, could move through it without a lamp — and turned toward his father's presence. He found it immediately: a wall thick and pulsing with old bruise-light, sick and humid, beating with the slow regularity of a man trying not to know something he already knows.</w:t></w:r></w:p><w:p w:rsidR="00000000" w:rsidDel="00000000" w:rsidP="00000000" w:rsidRDefault="00000000" w:rsidRPr="00000000" w14:paraId="0000001C"><w:pPr><w:rPr><w:rFonts w:ascii="EB Garamond" w:cs="EB Garamond" w:eastAsia="EB Garamond" w:hAnsi="EB Garamond"/><w:sz w:val="24"/><w:szCs w:val="24"/></w:rPr></w:pPr><w:r w:rsidDel="00000000" w:rsidR="00000000" w:rsidRPr="00000000"><w:rPr><w:rtl w:val="0"/></w:rPr></w:r></w:p><w:p w:rsidR="00000000" w:rsidDel="00000000" w:rsidP="00000000" w:rsidRDefault="00000000" w:rsidRPr="00000000" w14:paraId="0000001D"><w:pPr><w:rPr><w:rFonts w:ascii="EB Garamond" w:cs="EB Garamond" w:eastAsia="EB Garamond" w:hAnsi="EB Garamond"/><w:sz w:val="24"/><w:szCs w:val="24"/></w:rPr></w:pPr><w:r w:rsidDel="00000000" w:rsidR="00000000" w:rsidRPr="00000000"><w:rPr><w:rFonts w:ascii="EB Garamond" w:cs="EB Garamond" w:eastAsia="EB Garamond" w:hAnsi="EB Garamond"/><w:sz w:val="24"/><w:szCs w:val="24"/><w:rtl w:val="0"/></w:rPr><w:t xml:space="preserve">Something bled through that wall that was not Dan's. A child's scream cut short. Blood on white stone. A voice calling one name and stopping mid-syllable. The deep, bottomless shame of a man who cannot locate the moment he should have acted differently. Dan pressed his palm against the wall. Then he pressed his whole intention.</w:t></w:r></w:p><w:p w:rsidR="00000000" w:rsidDel="00000000" w:rsidP="00000000" w:rsidRDefault="00000000" w:rsidRPr="00000000" w14:paraId="0000001E"><w:pPr><w:rPr><w:rFonts w:ascii="EB Garamond" w:cs="EB Garamond" w:eastAsia="EB Garamond" w:hAnsi="EB Garamond"/><w:sz w:val="24"/><w:szCs w:val="24"/></w:rPr></w:pPr><w:r w:rsidDel="00000000" w:rsidR="00000000" w:rsidRPr="00000000"><w:rPr><w:rtl w:val="0"/></w:rPr></w:r></w:p><w:p w:rsidR="00000000" w:rsidDel="00000000" w:rsidP="00000000" w:rsidRDefault="00000000" w:rsidRPr="00000000" w14:paraId="0000001F"><w:pPr><w:rPr><w:rFonts w:ascii="EB Garamond" w:cs="EB Garamond" w:eastAsia="EB Garamond" w:hAnsi="EB Garamond"/><w:sz w:val="24"/><w:szCs w:val="24"/></w:rPr></w:pPr><w:r w:rsidDel="00000000" w:rsidR="00000000" w:rsidRPr="00000000"><w:rPr><w:rFonts w:ascii="EB Garamond" w:cs="EB Garamond" w:eastAsia="EB Garamond" w:hAnsi="EB Garamond"/><w:sz w:val="24"/><w:szCs w:val="24"/><w:rtl w:val="0"/></w:rPr><w:t xml:space="preserve">The wall broke — total and sudden as a held breath finally released — and Dan stumbled through into a wasteland beneath an ashen sky, where Father Aviel stood at the plain's center with his shoulders drawn inward and his arms wrapped around himself as if to keep his form, waiting in the posture of a man who has been waiting so long he has forgotten what he waits for. The ground was packed and dry. The sky held no feature.</w:t></w:r></w:p><w:p w:rsidR="00000000" w:rsidDel="00000000" w:rsidP="00000000" w:rsidRDefault="00000000" w:rsidRPr="00000000" w14:paraId="00000020"><w:pPr><w:rPr><w:rFonts w:ascii="EB Garamond" w:cs="EB Garamond" w:eastAsia="EB Garamond" w:hAnsi="EB Garamond"/><w:sz w:val="24"/><w:szCs w:val="24"/></w:rPr></w:pPr><w:r w:rsidDel="00000000" w:rsidR="00000000" w:rsidRPr="00000000"><w:rPr><w:rtl w:val="0"/></w:rPr></w:r></w:p><w:p w:rsidR="00000000" w:rsidDel="00000000" w:rsidP="00000000" w:rsidRDefault="00000000" w:rsidRPr="00000000" w14:paraId="00000021"><w:pPr><w:rPr><w:rFonts w:ascii="EB Garamond" w:cs="EB Garamond" w:eastAsia="EB Garamond" w:hAnsi="EB Garamond"/><w:sz w:val="24"/><w:szCs w:val="24"/></w:rPr></w:pPr><w:r w:rsidDel="00000000" w:rsidR="00000000" w:rsidRPr="00000000"><w:rPr><w:rFonts w:ascii="EB Garamond" w:cs="EB Garamond" w:eastAsia="EB Garamond" w:hAnsi="EB Garamond"/><w:sz w:val="24"/><w:szCs w:val="24"/><w:rtl w:val="0"/></w:rPr><w:t xml:space="preserve">Dan lifted his palm toward the emptiness above. Intent was enough: in the dreamscape, the body's vocabulary compresses to a single word, and that word is direction. Rocks descended from the motionless sky — not clean rain but hard mineral chunks, angular and indifferent. Aviel looked up. One struck his shoulder. He picked it up, and in the peculiar logic of the place it transformed in his hand — became gold, dense and cold, carrying the specific weight of what men will abandon their families for. His eyes went wide. More fell, each transforming: copper pieces, silver rings, gems holding colors too deep and too bright for anything belonging to daylight. Aviel began to gather with the frantic efficiency of a man who has been waiting his entire life for exactly this permission. The pile rose past his ankles, past his knees, past his waist, and still his hands would not stop trying to fill something with no floor.</w:t></w:r></w:p><w:p w:rsidR="00000000" w:rsidDel="00000000" w:rsidP="00000000" w:rsidRDefault="00000000" w:rsidRPr="00000000" w14:paraId="00000022"><w:pPr><w:rPr><w:rFonts w:ascii="EB Garamond" w:cs="EB Garamond" w:eastAsia="EB Garamond" w:hAnsi="EB Garamond"/><w:sz w:val="24"/><w:szCs w:val="24"/></w:rPr></w:pPr><w:r w:rsidDel="00000000" w:rsidR="00000000" w:rsidRPr="00000000"><w:rPr><w:rtl w:val="0"/></w:rPr></w:r></w:p><w:p w:rsidR="00000000" w:rsidDel="00000000" w:rsidP="00000000" w:rsidRDefault="00000000" w:rsidRPr="00000000" w14:paraId="00000023"><w:pPr><w:rPr><w:rFonts w:ascii="EB Garamond" w:cs="EB Garamond" w:eastAsia="EB Garamond" w:hAnsi="EB Garamond"/><w:sz w:val="24"/><w:szCs w:val="24"/></w:rPr></w:pPr><w:r w:rsidDel="00000000" w:rsidR="00000000" w:rsidRPr="00000000"><w:rPr><w:rFonts w:ascii="EB Garamond" w:cs="EB Garamond" w:eastAsia="EB Garamond" w:hAnsi="EB Garamond"/><w:sz w:val="24"/><w:szCs w:val="24"/><w:rtl w:val="0"/></w:rPr><w:t xml:space="preserve">Past his chest. Past his neck. Aviel was buried to the chin with his hands frozen mid-grasp, his fingers fused to the metal, his face cycling through dark red and then the deep purple of something being pressed closed from outside. His mouth opened in a silent accusation at the empty sky.</w:t></w:r></w:p><w:p w:rsidR="00000000" w:rsidDel="00000000" w:rsidP="00000000" w:rsidRDefault="00000000" w:rsidRPr="00000000" w14:paraId="00000024"><w:pPr><w:rPr><w:rFonts w:ascii="EB Garamond" w:cs="EB Garamond" w:eastAsia="EB Garamond" w:hAnsi="EB Garamond"/><w:sz w:val="24"/><w:szCs w:val="24"/></w:rPr></w:pPr><w:r w:rsidDel="00000000" w:rsidR="00000000" w:rsidRPr="00000000"><w:rPr><w:rtl w:val="0"/></w:rPr></w:r></w:p><w:p w:rsidR="00000000" w:rsidDel="00000000" w:rsidP="00000000" w:rsidRDefault="00000000" w:rsidRPr="00000000" w14:paraId="00000025"><w:pPr><w:rPr><w:rFonts w:ascii="EB Garamond" w:cs="EB Garamond" w:eastAsia="EB Garamond" w:hAnsi="EB Garamond"/><w:sz w:val="24"/><w:szCs w:val="24"/></w:rPr></w:pPr><w:r w:rsidDel="00000000" w:rsidR="00000000" w:rsidRPr="00000000"><w:rPr><w:rFonts w:ascii="EB Garamond" w:cs="EB Garamond" w:eastAsia="EB Garamond" w:hAnsi="EB Garamond"/><w:sz w:val="24"/><w:szCs w:val="24"/><w:rtl w:val="0"/></w:rPr><w:t xml:space="preserve">Dan held the image. Then raised his palm a final time. The wind that came was cold and total: it gathered everything into a spiral and scattered it all back into dust, and the dust dissolved as it fell. The plain emptied. Aviel knelt alone with his hands still shaped for holding. For one moment his eyes cleared — he looked up, and above him appeared a single star, small and cold as the truest things are. He stared at it with the expression of a man discovering he has been forgiven for something he never confessed. Something in him cracked — not broke entirely, but cracked enough to admit one thin shaft of light. Dan released his hold. The star faded. The plain dissolved. Aviel drifted down toward the deep current of dreamless sleep, and Dan let him go. From behind his father's door came the sound of a man sleeping deeply — the slow, unhurried draw of someone no longer running. Dan stood still and listened to it for a long moment. Then he kept on forward..</w:t></w:r></w:p><w:p w:rsidR="00000000" w:rsidDel="00000000" w:rsidP="00000000" w:rsidRDefault="00000000" w:rsidRPr="00000000" w14:paraId="00000026"><w:pPr><w:rPr><w:rFonts w:ascii="EB Garamond" w:cs="EB Garamond" w:eastAsia="EB Garamond" w:hAnsi="EB Garamond"/><w:sz w:val="24"/><w:szCs w:val="24"/></w:rPr></w:pPr><w:r w:rsidDel="00000000" w:rsidR="00000000" w:rsidRPr="00000000"><w:rPr><w:rtl w:val="0"/></w:rPr></w:r></w:p><w:p w:rsidR="00000000" w:rsidDel="00000000" w:rsidP="00000000" w:rsidRDefault="00000000" w:rsidRPr="00000000" w14:paraId="00000027"><w:pPr><w:rPr><w:rFonts w:ascii="EB Garamond" w:cs="EB Garamond" w:eastAsia="EB Garamond" w:hAnsi="EB Garamond"/><w:sz w:val="24"/><w:szCs w:val="24"/></w:rPr></w:pPr><w:r w:rsidDel="00000000" w:rsidR="00000000" w:rsidRPr="00000000"><w:rPr><w:rFonts w:ascii="EB Garamond" w:cs="EB Garamond" w:eastAsia="EB Garamond" w:hAnsi="EB Garamond"/><w:sz w:val="24"/><w:szCs w:val="24"/><w:rtl w:val="0"/></w:rPr><w:t xml:space="preserve">Dan gasped back into the dark corridor, stone against his knees, the smell a solid wall. The torch was out. He pressed one hand to the wall and began moving — feet testing each step, his body heavier than it had been, his legs uncertain beneath him, the cold of the floor working up through his soles with the thorough patience of cold that has been held in stone all night. He moved by feel until the warmth of the banked coals met his face before his eyes could find the glow. He touched the pitch to ember. The torch caught. He stood for a moment while his world spun slightly, holding the returned light.</w:t></w:r></w:p><w:p w:rsidR="00000000" w:rsidDel="00000000" w:rsidP="00000000" w:rsidRDefault="00000000" w:rsidRPr="00000000" w14:paraId="00000028"><w:pPr><w:rPr><w:rFonts w:ascii="EB Garamond" w:cs="EB Garamond" w:eastAsia="EB Garamond" w:hAnsi="EB Garamond"/><w:sz w:val="24"/><w:szCs w:val="24"/></w:rPr></w:pPr><w:r w:rsidDel="00000000" w:rsidR="00000000" w:rsidRPr="00000000"><w:rPr><w:rtl w:val="0"/></w:rPr></w:r></w:p><w:p w:rsidR="00000000" w:rsidDel="00000000" w:rsidP="00000000" w:rsidRDefault="00000000" w:rsidRPr="00000000" w14:paraId="00000029"><w:pPr><w:rPr><w:rFonts w:ascii="EB Garamond" w:cs="EB Garamond" w:eastAsia="EB Garamond" w:hAnsi="EB Garamond"/><w:sz w:val="24"/><w:szCs w:val="24"/></w:rPr></w:pPr><w:r w:rsidDel="00000000" w:rsidR="00000000" w:rsidRPr="00000000"><w:rPr><w:rFonts w:ascii="EB Garamond" w:cs="EB Garamond" w:eastAsia="EB Garamond" w:hAnsi="EB Garamond"/><w:sz w:val="24"/><w:szCs w:val="24"/><w:rtl w:val="0"/></w:rPr><w:t xml:space="preserve">Consider, before I describe what comes next, that there is nothing remarkable about a clean room. People have them everywhere. What is remarkable — what I watched for six years particularly near various preferred corners of this house, as I made a particular study of the boy — was a clean room maintained by deliberate act inside a house that was actively trying to become something else. While the western passage filled with crates and the eastern room became a storage argument the crates were losing, while the corridor between accumulated its purposeful mold and its astrolabes pointing toward nothing good, Dan's room had been maintained with a precision that spoke not of tidiness but of something closer to decision. The blanket lay folded with crisp corners. The floor was swept clean. His possessions arranged with careful economy: the clay cup, the wooden comb his mother had carved from a fig branch, the flint stylus for marking stone, and one small window kept clear so that morning light could enter, which it did, daily, whether or not anyone stood in it to receive it. He had maintained this order since the age of ten. Without instruction, without acknowledgment, without any particular expectation of reward. One might note — and I do note — that Dan had spent six years putting his own house in order before he ever dared concern himself with the disorder of the world immediately outside his door. The room was the hard-won proof of it. He would not have used those words. The room was there regardless, and it was ordered, and it mattered in ways he did not yet have language for.</w:t></w:r></w:p><w:p w:rsidR="00000000" w:rsidDel="00000000" w:rsidP="00000000" w:rsidRDefault="00000000" w:rsidRPr="00000000" w14:paraId="0000002A"><w:pPr><w:rPr><w:rFonts w:ascii="EB Garamond" w:cs="EB Garamond" w:eastAsia="EB Garamond" w:hAnsi="EB Garamond"/><w:sz w:val="24"/><w:szCs w:val="24"/></w:rPr></w:pPr><w:r w:rsidDel="00000000" w:rsidR="00000000" w:rsidRPr="00000000"><w:rPr><w:rtl w:val="0"/></w:rPr></w:r></w:p><w:p w:rsidR="00000000" w:rsidDel="00000000" w:rsidP="00000000" w:rsidRDefault="00000000" w:rsidRPr="00000000" w14:paraId="0000002B"><w:pPr><w:rPr><w:rFonts w:ascii="EB Garamond" w:cs="EB Garamond" w:eastAsia="EB Garamond" w:hAnsi="EB Garamond"/><w:sz w:val="24"/><w:szCs w:val="24"/></w:rPr></w:pPr><w:r w:rsidDel="00000000" w:rsidR="00000000" w:rsidRPr="00000000"><w:rPr><w:rFonts w:ascii="EB Garamond" w:cs="EB Garamond" w:eastAsia="EB Garamond" w:hAnsi="EB Garamond"/><w:sz w:val="24"/><w:szCs w:val="24"/><w:rtl w:val="0"/></w:rPr><w:t xml:space="preserve">His shadow however,  projected on a wall by the active flame, was hunched and wrong — the silhouette of a much older exhaustion compressed into a sixteen-year-old frame. He did not see it. He lowered himself onto the cot, pressed the torch into its crack in the wall — the flicker burning low but not yet done, not yet willing to concede — and lay back. Barely shaped into words, he said something to the ceiling stone that ended with &quot;Mother&quot; and contained the word &quot;stardust.&quot; The ceiling stone did not respond. He had not asked it to. The exhaustion took him without announcement, the way a tide takes what the shore has been holding: without particular violence, but with the absolute certainty of a process that has simply decided it is time.</w:t></w:r></w:p><w:p w:rsidR="00000000" w:rsidDel="00000000" w:rsidP="00000000" w:rsidRDefault="00000000" w:rsidRPr="00000000" w14:paraId="0000002C"><w:pPr><w:rPr><w:rFonts w:ascii="EB Garamond" w:cs="EB Garamond" w:eastAsia="EB Garamond" w:hAnsi="EB Garamond"/><w:sz w:val="24"/><w:szCs w:val="24"/></w:rPr></w:pPr><w:r w:rsidDel="00000000" w:rsidR="00000000" w:rsidRPr="00000000"><w:rPr><w:rtl w:val="0"/></w:rPr></w:r></w:p><w:p w:rsidR="00000000" w:rsidDel="00000000" w:rsidP="00000000" w:rsidRDefault="00000000" w:rsidRPr="00000000" w14:paraId="0000002D"><w:pPr><w:rPr><w:rFonts w:ascii="EB Garamond" w:cs="EB Garamond" w:eastAsia="EB Garamond" w:hAnsi="EB Garamond"/><w:sz w:val="24"/><w:szCs w:val="24"/></w:rPr></w:pPr><w:r w:rsidDel="00000000" w:rsidR="00000000" w:rsidRPr="00000000"><w:rPr><w:rFonts w:ascii="EB Garamond" w:cs="EB Garamond" w:eastAsia="EB Garamond" w:hAnsi="EB Garamond"/><w:sz w:val="24"/><w:szCs w:val="24"/><w:rtl w:val="0"/></w:rPr><w:t xml:space="preserve">What came was not rest. It was the sleep that seizes the body when the body has stopped asking the will for permission.</w:t></w:r></w:p><w:p w:rsidR="00000000" w:rsidDel="00000000" w:rsidP="00000000" w:rsidRDefault="00000000" w:rsidRPr="00000000" w14:paraId="0000002E"><w:pPr><w:rPr><w:rFonts w:ascii="EB Garamond" w:cs="EB Garamond" w:eastAsia="EB Garamond" w:hAnsi="EB Garamond"/><w:sz w:val="24"/><w:szCs w:val="24"/></w:rPr></w:pPr><w:r w:rsidDel="00000000" w:rsidR="00000000" w:rsidRPr="00000000"><w:rPr><w:rtl w:val="0"/></w:rPr></w:r></w:p><w:p w:rsidR="00000000" w:rsidDel="00000000" w:rsidP="00000000" w:rsidRDefault="00000000" w:rsidRPr="00000000" w14:paraId="0000002F"><w:pPr><w:rPr><w:rFonts w:ascii="EB Garamond" w:cs="EB Garamond" w:eastAsia="EB Garamond" w:hAnsi="EB Garamond"/><w:sz w:val="24"/><w:szCs w:val="24"/></w:rPr></w:pPr><w:r w:rsidDel="00000000" w:rsidR="00000000" w:rsidRPr="00000000"><w:rPr><w:rFonts w:ascii="EB Garamond" w:cs="EB Garamond" w:eastAsia="EB Garamond" w:hAnsi="EB Garamond"/><w:sz w:val="24"/><w:szCs w:val="24"/><w:rtl w:val="0"/></w:rPr><w:t xml:space="preserve">Dan found himself standing on water — bare feet on a surface that was liquid at one angle and solid at another, the way certain truths hold only when approached directly. The sky above held a blue found only in the country where waking and sleeping share a border — not a color belonging to daylight, carrying no equivalent in any mineral or dye or season. For a moment the stillness of the place offered something close to peace. He took one step. The water held. He took another.</w:t></w:r></w:p><w:p w:rsidR="00000000" w:rsidDel="00000000" w:rsidP="00000000" w:rsidRDefault="00000000" w:rsidRPr="00000000" w14:paraId="00000030"><w:pPr><w:rPr><w:rFonts w:ascii="EB Garamond" w:cs="EB Garamond" w:eastAsia="EB Garamond" w:hAnsi="EB Garamond"/><w:sz w:val="24"/><w:szCs w:val="24"/></w:rPr></w:pPr><w:r w:rsidDel="00000000" w:rsidR="00000000" w:rsidRPr="00000000"><w:rPr><w:rtl w:val="0"/></w:rPr></w:r></w:p><w:p w:rsidR="00000000" w:rsidDel="00000000" w:rsidP="00000000" w:rsidRDefault="00000000" w:rsidRPr="00000000" w14:paraId="00000031"><w:pPr><w:rPr><w:rFonts w:ascii="EB Garamond" w:cs="EB Garamond" w:eastAsia="EB Garamond" w:hAnsi="EB Garamond"/><w:sz w:val="24"/><w:szCs w:val="24"/></w:rPr></w:pPr><w:r w:rsidDel="00000000" w:rsidR="00000000" w:rsidRPr="00000000"><w:rPr><w:rFonts w:ascii="EB Garamond" w:cs="EB Garamond" w:eastAsia="EB Garamond" w:hAnsi="EB Garamond"/><w:sz w:val="24"/><w:szCs w:val="24"/><w:rtl w:val="0"/></w:rPr><w:t xml:space="preserve">Then it changed beneath him.
