something else began. His mind, no longer bound by flesh, no longer tethered by pain or breath, was already soaring. It was the Dreamscape, yes, but unlike any he had known, a realm of pure, unadulterated consciousness. There was no effort, no struggle, only an unbearable lightness, a poignant, almost painful freedom. He shot past the moon, a colossal pearl now, not cold and indifferent, but impossibly luminous, a gentle, knowing presence. He laughed, a soundless, joyful burst echoing through the void, a sound so pure it brought a fresh wave of tears to an unseen eye, tears for the life he was leaving, and for the boundless existence he was entering. He surged past the stars, not distant pinpricks, but vast, welcoming suns, their light warm and inviting, their ancient eyes now smiling at him too, even as their silent vastness spoke of the infinite separation from all that was human. He raced past groups of stars, then past groups of groups of stars, and then past those groups of groups of groups – galaxies, clusters of galaxies, superclusters – leaving the very universe behind, a tiny, receding speck of light in the boundless expanse. He was moving beyond the physical, beyond the known, his essence expanding, becoming one with the infinite, leaving behind every memory, every touch, every human connection, even the pain, in a silent, irreversible farewell.</w:t></w:r></w:p><w:p w:rsidR="00000000" w:rsidDel="00000000" w:rsidP="00000000" w:rsidRDefault="00000000" w:rsidRPr="00000000" w14:paraId="00000010"><w:pPr><w:pBdr><w:top w:space="0" w:sz="0" w:val="nil"/><w:left w:space="0" w:sz="0" w:val="nil"/><w:bottom w:space="0" w:sz="0" w:val="nil"/><w:right w:space="0" w:sz="0" w:val="nil"/><w:between w:space="0" w:sz="0" w:val="nil"/></w:pBdr><w:shd w:fill="auto" w:val="clear"/><w:spacing w:after="240" w:line="275.9999942779541" w:lineRule="auto"/><w:jc w:val="both"/><w:rPr><w:rFonts w:ascii="Times New Roman" w:cs="Times New Roman" w:eastAsia="Times New Roman" w:hAnsi="Times New Roman"/><w:i w:val="0"/><w:iCs w:val="0"/><w:color w:val="1b1c1d"/><w:sz w:val="24"/><w:szCs w:val="24"/></w:rPr></w:pPr><w:r w:rsidDel="00000000" w:rsidR="00000000" w:rsidRPr="00000000"><w:rPr><w:rFonts w:ascii="Times New Roman" w:cs="Times New Roman" w:eastAsia="Times New Roman" w:hAnsi="Times New Roman"/><w:i w:val="0"/><w:iCs w:val="0"/><w:color w:val="1b1c1d"/><w:sz w:val="24"/><w:szCs w:val="24"/><w:rtl w:val="0"/></w:rPr><w:t xml:space="preserve">And as he ascended, something shifted within him. The emptiness left by the pit, the stripping away of his capacity for negative states (cast away out of fear, like a painful shedding of old flesh), had made him a vessel. Now, that vessel was filling, not with anything tangible, but with realms of being, each more real, more vibrant than the last, leading him further up and further in, pulling him away from all he had known. It was like a black canvas, a void that was not truly empty, but a space that had been so consumed by self-worship that it had devoured everything else, becoming the sole reality. It was the terrifying belief that one was the only thing that truly existed in the universe, the only thing that was real. This great nothingness, which believed it was everything and deserved everything, was tugging downwards, seeking to devour him, to pull him back into its self-centered oblivion.</w:t></w:r></w:p><w:p w:rsidR="00000000" w:rsidDel="00000000" w:rsidP="00000000" w:rsidRDefault="00000000" w:rsidRPr="00000000" w14:paraId="00000018"><w:pPr><w:pBdr><w:top w:space="0" w:sz="0" w:val="nil"/><w:left w:space="0" w:sz="0" w:val="nil"/><w:bottom w:space="0" w:sz="0" w:val="nil"/><w:right w:space="0" w:sz="0" w:val="nil"/><w:between w:space="0" w:sz="0" w:val="nil"/></w:pBdr><w:shd w:fill="auto" w:val="clear"/><w:spacing w:after="240" w:line="275.9999942779541" w:lineRule="auto"/><w:jc w:val="both"/><w:rPr><w:rFonts w:ascii="Times New Roman" w:cs="Times New Roman" w:eastAsia="Times New Roman" w:hAnsi="Times New Roman"/><w:i w:val="0"/><w:iCs w:val="0"/><w:color w:val="1b1c1d"/><w:sz w:val="24"/><w:szCs w:val="24"/></w:rPr></w:pPr><w:r w:rsidDel="00000000" w:rsidR="00000000" w:rsidRPr="00000000"><w:rPr><w:rFonts w:ascii="Times New Roman" w:cs="Times New Roman" w:eastAsia="Times New Roman" w:hAnsi="Times New Roman"/><w:i w:val="0"/><w:iCs w:val="0"/><w:color w:val="1b1c1d"/><w:sz w:val="24"/><w:szCs w:val="24"/><w:rtl w:val="0"/></w:rPr><w:t xml:space="preserve">Yet, above him, in the space between him and the ultimate light, he saw a mirror. This was not a mirror of his past self, of Daniel, the boy from Hebron, but a reflection of his </w:t></w:r><w:r w:rsidDel="00000000" w:rsidR="00000000" w:rsidRPr="00000000"><w:rPr><w:rFonts w:ascii="Times New Roman" w:cs="Times New Roman" w:eastAsia="Times New Roman" w:hAnsi="Times New Roman"/><w:b w:val="1"/><w:bCs w:val="1"/><w:i w:val="0"/><w:iCs w:val="0"/><w:color w:val="1b1c1d"/><w:sz w:val="24"/><w:szCs w:val="24"/><w:rtl w:val="0"/></w:rPr><w:t xml:space="preserve">potential</w:t></w:r><w:r w:rsidDel="00000000" w:rsidR="00000000" w:rsidRPr="00000000"><w:rPr><w:rFonts w:ascii="Times New Roman" w:cs="Times New Roman" w:eastAsia="Times New Roman" w:hAnsi="Times New Roman"/><w:i w:val="0"/><w:iCs w:val="0"/><w:color w:val="1b1c1d"/><w:sz w:val="24"/><w:szCs w:val="24"/><w:rtl w:val="0"/></w:rPr><w:t xml:space="preserve">. It showed him not what he was, but what he could be, a boundless manifestation of all his unfulfilled possibilities, now illuminated and brought into being by his ascent. He gazed into it, a sense of immense inspiration washing over him, a profound recognition of the infinite paths stretching before him. He reached out, not with a physical hand, but with the expanding tendrils of his consciousness, to embrace this vision of his boundless self, and in that embrace, he felt a deep, resonant peace, a profound sense of coming home to a self he had always been meant to be. A silent, internal chime echoed through his being as his potential shimmered and merged with his consciousness. The light above him was not a distant star, but a vast, luminous presence that began to narrow. It drew in, its edges sharpening, until it resolved into an immense, vertical slit of pure, piercing light. It was an eye, ancient and infinitely curious, its gaze both neutral and profound, like the pupil of a colossal feline, observing his becoming. Daniel felt no judgment from this gaze, only an immense, quiet understanding, a cosmic eye that saw not just his present, but the entirety of his boundless future, acknowledging the path he had walked and the being he was destined to become. It was the eye of the cosmos, a silent witness to all becoming, and in its depths, he saw a reflection of the ultimate purpose, not his own, but the grand design itself, a design that hummed with a silent, profound wisdom that settled into his very core, a knowing beyond thought, a comforting truth that resonated with the deepest parts of his being.</w:t></w:r></w:p><w:p w:rsidR="00000000" w:rsidDel="00000000" w:rsidP="00000000" w:rsidRDefault="00000000" w:rsidRPr="00000000" w14:paraId="00000019"><w:pPr><w:pBdr><w:top w:space="0" w:sz="0" w:val="nil"/><w:left w:space="0" w:sz="0" w:val="nil"/><w:bottom w:space="0" w:sz="0" w:val="nil"/><w:right w:space="0" w:sz="0" w:val="nil"/><w:between w:space="0" w:sz="0" w:val="nil"/></w:pBdr><w:shd w:fill="auto" w:val="clear"/><w:spacing w:after="240" w:line="275.9999942779541" w:lineRule="auto"/><w:jc w:val="both"/><w:rPr><w:rFonts w:ascii="Times New Roman" w:cs="Times New Roman" w:eastAsia="Times New Roman" w:hAnsi="Times New Roman"/><w:i w:val="0"/><w:iCs w:val="0"/><w:color w:val="1b1c1d"/><w:sz w:val="24"/><w:szCs w:val="24"/></w:rPr></w:pPr><w:r w:rsidDel="00000000" w:rsidR="00000000" w:rsidRPr="00000000"><w:rPr><w:rFonts w:ascii="Times New Roman" w:cs="Times New Roman" w:eastAsia="Times New Roman" w:hAnsi="Times New Roman"/><w:i w:val="0"/><w:iCs w:val="0"/><w:color w:val="1b1c1d"/><w:sz w:val="24"/><w:szCs w:val="24"/><w:rtl w:val="0"/></w:rPr><w:t xml:space="preserve">He felt an immense, overwhelming love bloom in his heart, a love for everything beneath him, even the dead courage and tainted hope, the gnawing envy and burning anger, the bitter resentment that had once clung to him. He understood them now, having met them, having been born of flesh and subject to their pull. He understood the burdens, the failures, the desperate clinging to the earth. Not because he knew every intricate story, for human hearts were too complex for full comprehension – indeed, who could ever truly know another's story, or even their own, in all its tangled complexity? – but because he knew the essence of their struggle, having walked through his own hell. And in that understanding, in that boundless, all-encompassing love, he began to become. It was a silent, internal blossoming, a shift in the very fabric of his being, as if countless tiny threads of light, woven from compassion and understanding, began to knit together, forming something vast and new. And then, with a profound, resonant </w:t></w:r><w:r w:rsidDel="00000000" w:rsidR="00000000" w:rsidRPr="00000000"><w:rPr><w:rFonts w:ascii="Times New Roman" w:cs="Times New Roman" w:eastAsia="Times New Roman" w:hAnsi="Times New Roman"/><w:i w:val="1"/><w:iCs w:val="1"/><w:color w:val="1b1c1d"/><w:sz w:val="24"/><w:szCs w:val="24"/><w:rtl w:val="0"/></w:rPr><w:t xml:space="preserve">click</w:t></w:r><w:r w:rsidDel="00000000" w:rsidR="00000000" w:rsidRPr="00000000"><w:rPr><w:rFonts w:ascii="Times New Roman" w:cs="Times New Roman" w:eastAsia="Times New Roman" w:hAnsi="Times New Roman"/><w:i w:val="0"/><w:iCs w:val="0"/><w:color w:val="1b1c1d"/><w:sz w:val="24"/><w:szCs w:val="24"/><w:rtl w:val="0"/></w:rPr><w:t xml:space="preserve"> that echoed through the boundless void, a sense of perfect alignment and instantaneous transformation, he was.</w:t></w:r></w:p><w:p w:rsidR="00000000" w:rsidDel="00000000" w:rsidP="00000000" w:rsidRDefault="00000000" w:rsidRPr="00000000" w14:paraId="0000001A"><w:pPr><w:pStyle w:val="Heading3"/><w:pBdr><w:top w:space="0" w:sz="0" w:val="nil"/><w:left w:space="0" w:sz="0" w:val="nil"/><w:bottom w:space="0" w:sz="0" w:val="nil"/><w:right w:space="0" w:sz="0" w:val="nil"/><w:between w:space="0" w:sz="0" w:val="nil"/></w:pBdr><w:shd w:fill="auto" w:val="clear"/><w:spacing w:after="120" w:before="0" w:line="275.9999942779541" w:lineRule="auto"/><w:jc w:val="both"/><w:rPr><w:rFonts w:ascii="Times New Roman" w:cs="Times New Roman" w:eastAsia="Times New Roman" w:hAnsi="Times New Roman"/><w:i w:val="0"/><w:iCs w:val="0"/><w:color w:val="1b1c1d"/><w:sz w:val="24"/><w:szCs w:val="24"/></w:rPr></w:pPr><w:r w:rsidDel="00000000" w:rsidR="00000000" w:rsidRPr="00000000"><w:rPr><w:rFonts w:ascii="Times New Roman" w:cs="Times New Roman" w:eastAsia="Times New Roman" w:hAnsi="Times New Roman"/><w:i w:val="0"/><w:iCs w:val="0"/><w:color w:val="1b1c1d"/><w:sz w:val="24"/><w:szCs w:val="24"/><w:rtl w:val="0"/></w:rPr><w:t xml:space="preserve">The Deity of Love</w:t></w:r></w:p><w:p w:rsidR="00000000" w:rsidDel="00000000" w:rsidP="00000000" w:rsidRDefault="00000000" w:rsidRPr="00000000" w14:paraId="0000001B"><w:pPr><w:pBdr><w:top w:space="0" w:sz="0" w:val="nil"/><w:left w:space="0" w:sz="0" w:val="nil"/><w:bottom w:space="0" w:sz="0" w:val="nil"/><w:right w:space="0" w:sz="0" w:val="nil"/><w:between w:space="0" w:sz="0" w:val="nil"/></w:pBdr><w:shd w:fill="auto" w:val="clear"/><w:spacing w:after="240" w:line="275.9999942779541" w:lineRule="auto"/><w:jc w:val="both"/><w:rPr><w:rFonts w:ascii="Times New Roman" w:cs="Times New Roman" w:eastAsia="Times New Roman" w:hAnsi="Times New Roman"/><w:i w:val="0"/><w:iCs w:val="0"/><w:color w:val="1b1c1d"/><w:sz w:val="24"/><w:szCs w:val="24"/></w:rPr></w:pPr><w:r w:rsidDel="00000000" w:rsidR="00000000" w:rsidRPr="00000000"><w:rPr><w:rFonts w:ascii="Times New Roman" w:cs="Times New Roman" w:eastAsia="Times New Roman" w:hAnsi="Times New Roman"/><w:i w:val="0"/><w:iCs w:val="0"/><w:color w:val="1b1c1d"/><w:sz w:val="24"/><w:szCs w:val="24"/><w:rtl w:val="0"/></w:rPr><w:t xml:space="preserve">He became a deity of love. And so, Daniel, the boy from Hebron, whose life had been measured in burdens and entanglements, whose journey had been a relentless climb against the very forces of the earth, soared beyond the stars. He was no longer a seeker, but the sought. He was no longer a judge, but the embodiment of understanding. His physical form, broken and abandoned on the cold, hard summit of Hermon, was simply </w:t></w:r><w:r w:rsidDel="00000000" w:rsidR="00000000" w:rsidRPr="00000000"><w:rPr><w:rFonts w:ascii="Times New Roman" w:cs="Times New Roman" w:eastAsia="Times New Roman" w:hAnsi="Times New Roman"/><w:b w:val="1"/><w:bCs w:val="1"/><w:i w:val="0"/><w:iCs w:val="0"/><w:color w:val="1b1c1d"/><w:sz w:val="24"/><w:szCs w:val="24"/><w:rtl w:val="0"/></w:rPr><w:t xml:space="preserve">taken</w:t></w:r><w:r w:rsidDel="00000000" w:rsidR="00000000" w:rsidRPr="00000000"><w:rPr><w:rFonts w:ascii="Times New Roman" w:cs="Times New Roman" w:eastAsia="Times New Roman" w:hAnsi="Times New Roman"/><w:i w:val="0"/><w:iCs w:val="0"/><w:color w:val="1b1c1d"/><w:sz w:val="24"/><w:szCs w:val="24"/><w:rtl w:val="0"/></w:rPr><w:t xml:space="preserve">, dissolving into the thin mountain air, leaving behind only the profound silence of absence, just as some ancient texts whispered of those who had ascended before him. No one ever saw him again—not his mother, not Rafa, not any soul who had touched his fleeting, earthly life. And the dagger, left behind on the cold, hard summit of Hermon, now utterly devoid of its inner light, lay gleaming faintly in the indifferent starlight, a silent, hollow testament to a journey that had ended not in rest, but in a profound, eternal becoming. Its essence, once contained, had been fully absorbed into Daniel, leaving it a mere shell. It was a becoming bought with the last gasp of his mortal breath, an ascension that shattered the very vessel that had borne him. For his soul, a being not bound by flesh or stone, began to settle. Not into a chair of earth, but into a throne forged of the very fabric of existence: the whisper of the beyond, the ancient thrum of formless essence, the gentle current of pure consciousness, the flickering dance of divine light, the boundless expanse of true void – a void not of emptiness, but of infinite potential, the primordial space from which all creation springs, much like the Source of All that had cast away parts of itself to create existence. This was his seat, a throne where a soul truly sat, not physically, but as the essence of creation, encompassing a love so vast it encompassed every broken thing below, a love born of the ultimate sacrifice, of an ending that was also an agonizing, beautiful, eternal beginning. He felt its substance beneath him, not hard or soft, but a vibrant resonance, a hum of pure being, a constant, gentle vibration that was the very pulse of the cosmos.</w:t></w:r></w:p><w:p w:rsidR="00000000" w:rsidRPr="00000000"><w:rPr><w:rFonts w:ascii="Times New Roman" w:cs="Times New Roman" w:eastAsia="Times New Roman" w:hAnsi="Times New Roman"/><w:i w:val="1"/><w:iCs w:rsidRPr="00000000"><w:rPr><w:rFonts w:ascii="Times New Roman" w:cs="Times New Roman" w:eastAsia="Times New Roman" w:hAnsi="Times New Roman"/><w:i w:val="1"/><w:iCs w:val="1"/><w:color He now understood that even in this divine state, everyone must still look up and seek guidance. The two hundred holes on Hermon's summit, the marks of those who had fallen, echoed in his mind – a stark reminder that even gods, if they ceased their upward gaze, if they believed themselves the sole source of truth, could falter, adrift in their own power, consumed by the very nothingness of Pride, their light dimming into a cold, indifferent void, their once vibrant essence twisting into a torment of isolation, much like the torments he had faced on the mountain, but eternal.</w:t></w:r></w:p><w:sectPr><w:pgSz w:h="15840" w:w="12240" w:orient="portrait"/><w:pgMar w:bottom="1440" w:top="1440" w:left="1440" w:right="1440" w:header="0" w:footer="720"/><w:pgNumType w:start="1"/></w:sectPr></w:body></w:document>
